# Best BaseCRM Alternative – Top CRM Options for Businesses

Best BaseCRM Alternative: What to Choose in 2026
================================================

![Ethan Kim](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35369/ethan_kim-1.png)

Ethan Kim

Content Specialist

February 6, 2025

9 min read

Table of contents

*   [What is BaseCRM?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/basecrm/#title_0)
    
*   [Why Do People Look for a BaseCRM Alternative?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/basecrm/#title_1)
    
*   [How to Choose the Best Alternative](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/basecrm/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Best BaseCRM Alternative: What to Choose in 2026](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35351/basecrm.776x0.webp)

If you’ve been using BaseCRM (now called Zendesk Sell) and feel like it’s not quite right for your business anymore, it is quite common nowadays. Lots of BaseCRM clients have run into issues that make them look elsewhere.

But before switching to another CRM, it is helpful to know exactly why BaseCRM isn’t working for you and what to look for in a better option.

What is BaseCRM?
----------------

BaseCRM started as a simple, sales-focused CRM designed to help teams track leads, manage opportunities, and stay organized. It was later acquired by Zendesk and rebranded as Zendesk Sell. The idea was to integrate it into Zendesk’s suite of customer service tools, making it a more complete solution.

But just because a tool is part of a bigger system doesn’t mean it’s the best fit for every sales team. And that’s where the frustration comes in.

Why Do People Look for a BaseCRM Alternative?
---------------------------------------------

Based on real user reviews on platforms like G2 and Capterra, here are some of the biggest complaints about BaseCRM:

*   **Limited customization** – Many users feel stuck with the default setup and wish they could adjust workflows, fields, and reports to match how their sales teams actually work.
*   **Pricing concerns** – Some say the cost has increased over time without significant improvements to core product features.
*   **Lack of flexibility** – It’s built for sales teams, but businesses that need deeper automation, reporting, or integration with other tools often feel restricted.
*   **Customer support issues** – Several clients mention slow response times and unhelpful support when they run into problems.
*   **Features that feel basic** – Some teams outgrow the platform because they need more advanced reporting, automation, or pipeline management than what BaseCRM offers.

If any of these sound familiar, it might be time to switch. But picking the right alternative isn’t just about finding a CRM with more features.

How to Choose the Best Alternative
----------------------------------

There’s no perfect CRM—just one that fits your team better. Here are a few things to keep in mind when making the switch:

*   **Ease of use** – A CRM should make life easier and not more complex. If your team struggles to adopt this sales software, it is 99% they won’t use it.
*   **Customization options** – Your sales process isn’t the same as everyone else’s. A good BaseCRM alternative should let you tweak workflows, fields, and reports to match how your team works.
*   **Integration with other tools** – If your team relies on email, calendars, marketing tools, or customer support platforms, make sure [your new CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/features/crm/)
     connects with them smoothly.
*   **Automation and reporting** – Look for a system that reduces manual work and gives you clear insights into your pipeline, performance, and forecasts.
*   **Customer support** – No CRM is perfect. What matters is how quickly and effectively their support team helps when things go wrong.

Switching CRMs isn’t just about fixing frustrations—it’s about making sure your sales team has the right tools to close deals more efficiently. Before you make a decision, test out a few alternatives, see how they fit with your workflow, and ensure your team feels comfortable using them.

Simple and Easy-to-Use CRM Programs Like BaseCRM
------------------------------------------------

![Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34119/flowlu.png)

Flowlu

CRM That Connects Everything

[Try For Free](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

Flowlu is an all-in-one platform that brings together CRM, project management, client portal, knowledge base, and financial tracking tools. The CRM includes features for sales process automation and gives businesses a clear view of their customer journey. Multiple sales pipelines and custom fields make it adaptable for tracking leads and opportunities. It supports contact management, email and telephony integration, and automated workflows. Built-in web forms allow you to capture leads effortlessly. Customizable dashboards and detailed reports help you monitor performance and stay organized. Flowlu integrates with numerous third-party apps, allowing you to integrate it with your existing tools effortlessly. The platform is recognized for its cost-effectiveness, making it an attractive option for small and medium-sized businesses. Its feature set and pricing provide a practical solution for teams seeking a reliable client management tool.

![Zoho CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34120/zoho.png)

Zoho CRM

AI-Powered Client Connections

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/zoho-crm/)

Zoho CRM helps you stay connected with your clients by using AI to automate your CRM processes. It has tools for managing deals, contacts, and workflows. You can communicate with prospects directly through email, phone, or social media. Beyond the basics, it offers advanced features like business intelligence and sales forecasting. However, some users find it has a steep learning curve, higher costs, and slower support.

![Hubspot](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34121/hubspot.png)

Hubspot

Real-Time Customer Insights

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/hubspot/)

HubSpot is a well-known CRM that lets you track sales activity in real time. It’s great for building a central spot to store all your customer info. You get email tracking, handy templates, and integrations with Gmail and Outlook. Also, you can create custom dashboards, dig into analytics, and handle email campaigns like a pro. It’s got forms for lead capturing, plus segmentation tools and even chatbots to keep things moving. For solo entrepreneurs and small businesses, the cost can really sting. So, while it’s got bells and whistles, it might not fit every budget.

![SuiteDash](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34122/suitedash.png)

SuiteDash

Centralized Sales & Service

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/suitedash/)

SuiteDash calls itself a client portal software but also includes a CRM with tools for managing sales funnels. It helps you keep all client information in one place and offers automation features like scheduling follow-ups and creating new contacts automatically. You can choose from four different styles to monitor pipelines from various aspects. It also provides client onboarding tools like forms, checklists, and proposals. However, users often mention the platform feels slow and overwhelming during setup due to the many modules and unclear connections between them.

![Freshsales](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34123/freshsales.png)

Freshsales

Simplified AI-Driven Selling

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/)

Freshsales is an AI-powered CRM to make selling easier. Its Freddy AI helps you run targeted campaigns, qualify leads with intent scores, and gives you useful recommendations. The platform is all about automation to reduce manual tasks and features a simple interface. Freshsales provides customizable sales reports, and tools to create outreach campaigns. It integrates with email, messaging apps, SMS, Zoom, chat, and phone systems. There's also a marketplace for connecting with hundreds of popular apps. However, many users find the reporting and analysis tools overly complex, which can be a significant drawback.

![Vtiger](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34124/vtiger_2x.png)

Vtiger

Advanced CRM Made Simple

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/vtiger/)

Vtiger is a user-friendly CRM that connects with over 500 apps. It includes advanced tools for lead routing and prospect segmentation. The mobile app is especially handy, supporting voice notes and business card scanning. Vtiger also offers email marketing features, automated email sequences, and integrations with phone, chat, SMS, and websites. It helps you create forecast scenarios and predict estimated sales revenue. Still, many users mention that customer support could be improved.

![SugarCRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34126/sugarcrm.jpg)

SugarCRM

Custom Dashboards, Seamless Automation

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/sugarcrm/)

SugarCRM offers tools for sale and marketing management, and customer support. This software is very adaptable and lets you create dashboards and reports that match your company’s needs. Automation features save time on routine tasks. SugarCRM integrates well with accounting tools, messengers, ERP and so on. While it offers plenty of features, some users find the interface tricky and the learning curve steep.

![Agile CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34127/agile_crm_2x.png)

Agile CRM

Integrated Sales and Marketing

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/agilecrm/)

Agile CRM combines sales tracking, marketing, and customer service into one platform. It’s geared toward SMBs and includes tools like email campaigns and deal tracking. Agile CRM gives business tools for predictive analytics and sales forecasting, plus it offers multiple views. The free plan is a bonus for startups. Many users praise its affordability and variety of features. On the downside, performance can dip with larger datasets.

![Streak](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34128/streak_2x.png)

Streak

CRM Inside Your Inbox

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/streak/)

Streak works directly in Gmail, so there’s no need to switch between platforms. It helps you manage contacts and track sales within your inbox. The tool supports email tracking, templates, and integrations with Google apps. It’s great for keeping things simple. It has filters, sorting and grouping options to keep your data clean. That said, users report that the mobile version lacks some desktop features.

![Copper](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34129/copper_2x.png)

Copper

Google Workspace CRM Partner

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/copper/)

Copper works within Google Workspace, letting you manage sales and leads right from Gmail. It simplifies tasks like contact tracking and pipeline updates. Many users like its clean, intuitive design. You can also set up the workflow automation, so Copper will do the tasks for you. However, its reporting tools can feel basic. Some also mention limitations in the mobile app.

![Nimble](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34130/nimble.png)

Nimble

Social Media-Enhanced CRM

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/nimble/)

Nimble merges CRM with social media. You can track contacts, monitor conversations, and manage email outreach all in one place. It stands out for its ability to connect with various platforms. Nimble also offers ready-made templates with pre-defined fields and stages for your workflows. That said, reporting options are fairly minimal. This can be a drawback for users who need deeper insights.

![Nutshell](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34131/nutshell.png)

Nutshell

CRM for Growing Teams

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/nutshell/)

Nutshell caters to small businesses with features for email integration, sales automation, and team collaboration. The interface is straightforward, and the pricing fits tighter budgets. Nutshell provides native integrations, connections through 3rd-party apps and AppConnect. On the downside, its customization options are limited. Larger teams might outgrow it quickly.

![Salesforce](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34132/salesforce.png)

Salesforce

Scalable Power for Teams

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/salesforce/)

Salesforce is packed with features for managing sales, customer support, and marketing. It’s ideal for big teams needing scalable tools. Salesforce is integrated with AI, which means it can write emails for you, generate summaries, or provide real-time predictions, among other options. But all those features come at a price. Many users also mention the platform feels overwhelming without proper training.

![Insightly](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34133/insightly.png)

Insightly

CRM Meets Project Management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/)

Insightly combines CRM and project management. It’s handy for keeping track of both customers and tasks in one system. Insightly includes business process automation to free your team from routine tasks. You can also create real-time reports to visualize your sales data. The integration options are useful, but the interface feels clunky to some. Reporting tools, while functional, are less detailed than what many users expect.

![Pipedrive](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34134/pipedrive.png)

Pipedrive

Visual Pipelines, Simplified Sales

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/pipedrive/)

Pipedrive focuses on keeping sales organized. It uses a clean, visual pipeline to track deals and activities. You can automate tasks like follow-ups and reminders, and save time and efforts for your team. Email integration makes communication easy. Pipedrive also offers automated sales conversations with AI-powered prompts and insights. Users like its simplicity and find it easy to pick up. However, reporting features feel basic to some. Perhaps, Pipedrive could benefit if they implement more in-depth analytics for their clients.

![Bitrix24](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34135/bitrix24.png)

Bitrix24

Collaboration Meets CRM

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/bitrix24/)

Bitrix24 offers a versatile mix of CRM, communication, and project management tools. It lets teams collaborate through video calls, email tracking, and shared workspaces. Automated notifications via SMS or email can be set up to keep clients informed without manual effort. An AI assistant is included to help generate marketing content and brainstorm ideas, saving time for your team. Many users appreciate the wide range of tools available. It’s affordable, especially for smaller teams. On the downside, the interface can be overwhelming. Slow system performance is another common complaint.

![Kommo](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34136/kommo_2x.png)

Kommo

Messaging-Based Sales Simplified

Kommo focuses on messaging-based sales. It connects directly with apps like WhatsApp, Messenger, and Telegram. This makes real-time communication with leads simple. You can automate follow-ups and track deals easily. Kommo’s AI features help you to create summaries of your conversations or suggest replies. The interface is clean, and most features are intuitive. Customization, though, is somewhat limited. Support response times could also be better.

![EngageBay](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34137/engagebay_2x.png)

EngageBay

Affordable All-In-One CRM

EngageBay is a great option for small businesses. It incorporates marketing, sales, and service tools into one platform. EngageBay’s main features include email campaigns, lead generation, and a helpdesk. You can integrate SMS, track opens and link clicks. The free plan makes it appealing to startups. Many users like its range of options. Some, however, report bugs in the system. The learning curve can also be steep for beginners.

![Less Annoying CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34138/less_annoying_crm_2x.png)

Less Annoying CRM

Simplified CRM for SMBs

Less Annoying CRM keeps things simple. It’s built for small businesses that need basic tools. Contact and task management are easy to set up. The interface is clean, with no unnecessary extras. It’s affordable too, which users love. It integrates well with calendar apps, email, invoicing software, VOIP, web forms, video communication tools and other software you may utilize. That said, it lacks advanced features. There’s also no dedicated app for mobile use.

![monday Sales CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34139/monday_sales_crm_2x.png)

monday Sales CRM

Customizable Sales Tracking

monday Sales CRM offers flexibility for tracking deals and managing contacts. Built on the monday.com platform, it integrates easily with other tools. Automation options help reduce repetitive tasks. Teams enjoy the drag-and-drop customization. Its AI features let you generate tasks, compose emails, summarize calls and so much more. However, the setup process can take time. For smaller businesses, the price might feel high. Some users also find it overly reliant on custom workflows.

![NetHunt](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34140/nethunt_2x.png)

NetHunt

CRM Inside Gmail Workflow

NetHunt turns Gmail into a CRM. It allows you to manage deals and automate workflows without leaving your inbox. Email tracking and follow-ups are straightforward. It’s great for teams already using Google Workspace. NetHunt also includes reminders, calendar sync, and mentions for team collaboration. Many users praise its seamless integration. On mobile, though, certain features are missing. Larger teams may also feel it lacks advanced reporting.

![Pipeline](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34141/pipeline_2x.png)

Pipeline

Organized Sales Made Easy

Pipeline CRM simplifies sales processes. It tracks deals, contacts, and tasks in one place. Reporting tools offer basic insights into team performance. The interface is user-friendly and doesn’t overwhelm. Pipeline CRm automates workflows, sends reminders to your colleagues and suggests next steps. Customer service is quick and helpful, which users appreciate. Still, customization is limited. Reports could also use more depth for detailed analysis.

![Salesflare](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34142/salesflare_2x.png)

Salesflare

Automated CRM for SMBs

Salesflare is a CRM specialized in automating data entry for SMBs. It tracks emails, calls, and meetings automatically. The system also pulls contact details from online sources, saving users time. Many appreciate its simple setup and clean interface. Email tracking and pipeline management are popular features. However, customization options are limited. Some users feel the mobile app lacks important features found in the desktop version. For smaller teams, the software is a great fit and meets most needs. Larger companies, however, may find it too limiting for more complex workflows.

![Creatio CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34143/creatio-1.png)

Creatio CRM

CRM with Process Automation

Creatio blends CRM tools with process automation in one platform. It simplifies lead management, tracks sales, and handles workflows. The system adapts to fit various business needs. Users praise its flexibility. Teams can build unique solutions quickly. However, setup can be overwhelming due to its many options. New users often report needing additional training to get started. Once configured, it’s reliable for streamlining operations. Advanced reporting tools make it appealing to data-driven teams. On the downside, customer support response times could improve.

![Close](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34144/close_2x.png)

Close

Unified Communication Hub

Close brings email, SMS, and calls together in one place. It simplifies how teams connect with prospects and leads. A single inbox keeps all conversations organized. Close’s automation tools help with follow-ups and reminders. Reporting tools offer insights into team performance. Users find it straightforward to set up and use. However, some find the pricing steep for smaller teams. Customization is another area where it falls short. For teams that prioritize communication, it’s a solid choice.

![Capsule](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34145/capsule_2x.png)

Capsule

Essentials for Small Business

Capsule CRM focuses on simplicity and small business needs. It organizes contacts, tracks sales, and manages tasks in one place. Users like its straightforward design and affordable pricing. It integrates well with tools like Xero and Mailchimp. However, advanced features like workflow automation are missing. Reporting tools are basic but sufficient for smaller teams. The mobile app works well but could include more features. It’s a good option for businesses needing the essentials without the clutter.

![Breakcold](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34146/breakcold_2x.png)

Breakcold

CRM Meets Social Selling

Breakcold combines CRM functions with social selling tools. It tracks interactions on LinkedIn, Twitter, and email in one platform. Users can engage prospects directly from the dashboard. It’s great for teams focused on relationship-building. However, traditional CRM features like deal tracking and task automation are limited. The interface can also feel clunky to new users. Social media-driven teams appreciate its niche approach. For those needing broader functionality, it might not be the best fit.

![Folk](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34147/folk_2x.png)

Folk

Collaborative CRM for Teams

Folk is a modern CRM that emphasizes collaboration and simplicity. It lets teams manage contacts, assign tasks, and track interactions in one place. The interface is minimalistic and easy to navigate. It’s especially useful for small teams managing shared responsibilities. However, it lacks some advanced features seen in older CRMs. Integration options are still growing, which may limit its appeal. Bugs are occasionally reported by users. It works best for startups and smaller teams.

![CRMOne](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34148/crmone_2x.png)

CRMOne

Streamline Customer Interactions

CRMOne provides basic tools for contact management, sales tracking, and marketing automation. It’s designed to cover the essentials without overwhelming users. Many appreciate its straightforward interface. However, reviews suggest that the features are limited compared to competitors. Advanced analytics and integration options are lacking. Some users feel it’s best suited for teams with very simple needs. It’s an option for those who value simplicity over depth.

![BigContacts](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34149/bigcontacts_2x.png)

BigContacts

Contact Management Simplified

BigContacts focuses on contact management and email marketing for small to medium businesses. It features automated follow-ups and simple email templates. Users like its intuitive layout and helpful support team. However, reporting tools are basic and may not satisfy data-heavy teams. The mobile app could also benefit from additional features. It’s a solid choice for businesses that prioritize email campaigns and client organization. For more complex workflows, it may fall short.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

What is the best BaseCRM alternative for businesses?

The best BaseCRM replacement depends on your needs. Some CRM tools focus on small businesses looking for a simple and affordable solution, while others offer advanced automation and reporting for larger teams.

Are there any free CRM programs similar to BaseCRM?

Yes, several free CRM software options provide basic sales tracking, client management, and deal pipelines. However, most free plans have limits on users, storage, or features.

What CRM software works on Mac, Windows, and mobile apps?

Most modern CRMs are cloud-based, meaning they work on Mac, Windows, and mobile apps for Android and iOS. Some even offer offline access for when you're on the go.

# Best Copper CRM Alternatives: Top Options for SMBs

Affordable Alternatives to Copper CRM for Small Businesses
==========================================================

![Oliver Carter](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35461/oliver_carter-1.png)

Oliver Carter

Copywriting Specialist

February 7, 2025

9 min read

Table of contents

*   [What is Copper CRM?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/copper/#title_0)
    
*   [The Limitations of Copper CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/copper/#title_1)
    
*   [How to Choose the Best Alternative](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/copper/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Affordable Alternatives to Copper CRM for Small Businesses](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35443/copper.776x0.webp)

Choosing a CRM is a big deal. It’s the nerve center of your sales, customer interactions, and team collaboration. Copper CRM, with its strong Google Workspace integration, has been a go-to for many businesses.

But, frankly, it’s not the perfect fit for everyone. If you’re exploring alternatives, here’s what you should consider for your new CRM solution.

What is Copper CRM?
-------------------

Copper CRM is a customer relationship management tool built primarily for businesses that rely heavily on Google Workspace. It automatically logs emails, syncs contacts, and tracks sales pipelines without forcing users to switch between multiple apps. The promise? A smooth, familiar experience that feels like an extension of Gmail and Google Drive.

The Limitations of Copper CRM
-----------------------------

Despite its benefits, Copper has some notable drawbacks—many of which have been voiced by real customers on platforms like Capterra, G2, Crozdesk, and Trustpilot. Here are the biggest concerns we found there:

*   **Customer Support Woes** – Users have called out slow response times and unhelpful support. Some even mentioned frustrating experiences when trying to cancel their Copper subscriptions.
*   **Price vs. Features** – While it’s marketed as a lightweight, user-friendly CRM, many businesses feel they’re not getting enough value for the price they’re paying. Essential features, like advanced reporting, can feel limited for some clients.
*   **Locked into Google** – The deep Google Workspace integration is great if your business revolves around Gmail and Google Drive. But if you use other tools as well, like accounting or task tracking, you might feel boxed in.
*   **Limited Customization** – Some customers find it difficult to tailor the CRM to their specific business needs, especially when compared to more flexible alternatives with customizable views and fields.

How to Choose the Best Alternative
----------------------------------

If Copper CRM isn’t cutting it for you, you need to find the right alternative focusing on what really matters for your business. Here’s how to approach it:

*   **Identify Must-Have Features** – Need automation? Better reporting? [Stronger integrations](https://www.flowlu.com/integrations/)
    ? Make a list of what Copper was missing for you.
*   **Check User Reviews** – Sites like Capterra, G2, GetApp are goldmines of honest feedback. Look for patterns in what people love and hate about a CRM.
*   **Think About Scalability** – A CRM should work for you now, but also as your business grows. Make sure the tool you choose won’t hold you back in a year or two.
*   **Test Before You Commit** – Free trials exist for a reason. Before switching, take your top choices for a test run and see which one actually improves your workflow.

When looking for the right CRM focus not only on the features but more on how well it fits into your day-to-day business operations. The good news? There are plenty of [great CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/features/crm/)
 alternatives out there for entrepreneurs and teams. You just need to find the one that works best for you and your coworkers.

Best Copper CRM Competitors and Alternatives to Consider
--------------------------------------------------------

![Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34119/flowlu.png)

Flowlu

CRM That Connects Everything

[Try For Free](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

Flowlu is an all-in-one platform that brings together CRM, project management, client portal, knowledge base, and financial tracking tools. The CRM includes features for sales process automation and gives businesses a clear view of their customer journey. Multiple sales pipelines and custom fields make it adaptable for tracking leads and opportunities. It supports contact management, email and telephony integration, and automated workflows. Built-in web forms allow you to capture leads effortlessly. Customizable dashboards and detailed reports help you monitor performance and stay organized. Flowlu integrates with numerous third-party apps, allowing you to integrate it with your existing tools effortlessly. The platform is recognized for its cost-effectiveness, making it an attractive option for small and medium-sized businesses. Its feature set and pricing provide a practical solution for teams seeking a reliable client management tool.

![Zoho CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34120/zoho.png)

Zoho CRM

AI-Powered Client Connections

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/zoho-crm/)

Zoho CRM helps you stay connected with your clients by using AI to automate your CRM processes. It has tools for managing deals, contacts, and workflows. You can communicate with prospects directly through email, phone, or social media. Beyond the basics, it offers advanced features like business intelligence and sales forecasting. However, some users find it has a steep learning curve, higher costs, and slower support.

![Hubspot](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34121/hubspot.png)

Hubspot

Real-Time Customer Insights

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/hubspot/)

HubSpot is a well-known CRM that lets you track sales activity in real time. It’s great for building a central spot to store all your customer info. You get email tracking, handy templates, and integrations with Gmail and Outlook. Also, you can create custom dashboards, dig into analytics, and handle email campaigns like a pro. It’s got forms for lead capturing, plus segmentation tools and even chatbots to keep things moving. For solo entrepreneurs and small businesses, the cost can really sting. So, while it’s got bells and whistles, it might not fit every budget.

![SuiteDash](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34122/suitedash.png)

SuiteDash

Centralized Sales & Service

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/suitedash/)

SuiteDash calls itself a client portal software but also includes a CRM with tools for managing sales funnels. It helps you keep all client information in one place and offers automation features like scheduling follow-ups and creating new contacts automatically. You can choose from four different styles to monitor pipelines from various aspects. It also provides client onboarding tools like forms, checklists, and proposals. However, users often mention the platform feels slow and overwhelming during setup due to the many modules and unclear connections between them.

![Freshsales](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34123/freshsales.png)

Freshsales

Simplified AI-Driven Selling

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/)

Freshsales is an AI-powered CRM to make selling easier. Its Freddy AI helps you run targeted campaigns, qualify leads with intent scores, and gives you useful recommendations. The platform is all about automation to reduce manual tasks and features a simple interface. Freshsales provides customizable sales reports, and tools to create outreach campaigns. It integrates with email, messaging apps, SMS, Zoom, chat, and phone systems. There's also a marketplace for connecting with hundreds of popular apps. However, many users find the reporting and analysis tools overly complex, which can be a significant drawback.

![Vtiger](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34124/vtiger_2x.png)

Vtiger

Advanced CRM Made Simple

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/vtiger/)

Vtiger is a user-friendly CRM that connects with over 500 apps. It includes advanced tools for lead routing and prospect segmentation. The mobile app is especially handy, supporting voice notes and business card scanning. Vtiger also offers email marketing features, automated email sequences, and integrations with phone, chat, SMS, and websites. It helps you create forecast scenarios and predict estimated sales revenue. Still, many users mention that customer support could be improved.

![BaseCRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34125/basecrm_2x.png)

BaseCRM

Effortless Sales Simplification

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/basecrm/)

BaseCRM, now Zendesk Sell, focuses on simplifying sales tracking. It helps organize leads, track deals and activities, and automate repetitive tasks. You can also set up automation rules and triggers to streamline client journeys. Email tools and mobile access make it useful for teams on the move. Users appreciate its clean interface and reporting options. Still, some customers occasionally complain about slow customer service responses.

![SugarCRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34126/sugarcrm.jpg)

SugarCRM

Custom Dashboards, Seamless Automation

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/sugarcrm/)

SugarCRM offers tools for sale and marketing management, and customer support. This software is very adaptable and lets you create dashboards and reports that match your company’s needs. Automation features save time on routine tasks. SugarCRM integrates well with accounting tools, messengers, ERP and so on. While it offers plenty of features, some users find the interface tricky and the learning curve steep.

![Agile CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34127/agile_crm_2x.png)

Agile CRM

Integrated Sales and Marketing

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/agilecrm/)

Agile CRM combines sales tracking, marketing, and customer service into one platform. It’s geared toward SMBs and includes tools like email campaigns and deal tracking. Agile CRM gives business tools for predictive analytics and sales forecasting, plus it offers multiple views. The free plan is a bonus for startups. Many users praise its affordability and variety of features. On the downside, performance can dip with larger datasets.

![Streak](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34128/streak_2x.png)

Streak

CRM Inside Your Inbox

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/streak/)

Streak works directly in Gmail, so there’s no need to switch between platforms. It helps you manage contacts and track sales within your inbox. The tool supports email tracking, templates, and integrations with Google apps. It’s great for keeping things simple. It has filters, sorting and grouping options to keep your data clean. That said, users report that the mobile version lacks some desktop features.

![Nimble](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34130/nimble.png)

Nimble

Social Media-Enhanced CRM

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/nimble/)

Nimble merges CRM with social media. You can track contacts, monitor conversations, and manage email outreach all in one place. It stands out for its ability to connect with various platforms. Nimble also offers ready-made templates with pre-defined fields and stages for your workflows. That said, reporting options are fairly minimal. This can be a drawback for users who need deeper insights.

![Nutshell](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34131/nutshell.png)

Nutshell

CRM for Growing Teams

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/nutshell/)

Nutshell caters to small businesses with features for email integration, sales automation, and team collaboration. The interface is straightforward, and the pricing fits tighter budgets. Nutshell provides native integrations, connections through 3rd-party apps and AppConnect. On the downside, its customization options are limited. Larger teams might outgrow it quickly.

![Salesforce](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34132/salesforce.png)

Salesforce

Scalable Power for Teams

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/salesforce/)

Salesforce is packed with features for managing sales, customer support, and marketing. It’s ideal for big teams needing scalable tools. Salesforce is integrated with AI, which means it can write emails for you, generate summaries, or provide real-time predictions, among other options. But all those features come at a price. Many users also mention the platform feels overwhelming without proper training.

![Insightly](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34133/insightly.png)

Insightly

CRM Meets Project Management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/)

Insightly combines CRM and project management. It’s handy for keeping track of both customers and tasks in one system. Insightly includes business process automation to free your team from routine tasks. You can also create real-time reports to visualize your sales data. The integration options are useful, but the interface feels clunky to some. Reporting tools, while functional, are less detailed than what many users expect.

![Pipedrive](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34134/pipedrive.png)

Pipedrive

Visual Pipelines, Simplified Sales

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/pipedrive/)

Pipedrive focuses on keeping sales organized. It uses a clean, visual pipeline to track deals and activities. You can automate tasks like follow-ups and reminders, and save time and efforts for your team. Email integration makes communication easy. Pipedrive also offers automated sales conversations with AI-powered prompts and insights. Users like its simplicity and find it easy to pick up. However, reporting features feel basic to some. Perhaps, Pipedrive could benefit if they implement more in-depth analytics for their clients.

![Bitrix24](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34135/bitrix24.png)

Bitrix24

Collaboration Meets CRM

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/bitrix24/)

Bitrix24 offers a versatile mix of CRM, communication, and project management tools. It lets teams collaborate through video calls, email tracking, and shared workspaces. Automated notifications via SMS or email can be set up to keep clients informed without manual effort. An AI assistant is included to help generate marketing content and brainstorm ideas, saving time for your team. Many users appreciate the wide range of tools available. It’s affordable, especially for smaller teams. On the downside, the interface can be overwhelming. Slow system performance is another common complaint.

![Kommo](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34136/kommo_2x.png)

Kommo

Messaging-Based Sales Simplified

Kommo focuses on messaging-based sales. It connects directly with apps like WhatsApp, Messenger, and Telegram. This makes real-time communication with leads simple. You can automate follow-ups and track deals easily. Kommo’s AI features help you to create summaries of your conversations or suggest replies. The interface is clean, and most features are intuitive. Customization, though, is somewhat limited. Support response times could also be better.

However, it lacks some advanced features seen in older CRMs. Integration options are still growing, which may limit its appeal. Bugs are occasionally reported by users. ![CRMOne](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34148/crmone_2x.png)

CRMOne

Streamline Customer Interactions

CRMOne provides basic tools for contact management, sales tracking, and marketing automation. It’s designed to cover the essentials without overwhelming users. Many appreciate its straightforward interface. However, reviews suggest that the features are limited compared to competitors. Advanced analytics and integration options are lacking. Some users feel it’s best suited for teams with very simple needs. It’s an option for those who value simplicity over depth.

![BigContacts](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34149/bigcontacts_2x.png)

BigContacts

Contact Management Simplified

BigContacts focuses on contact management and email marketing for small to medium businesses. It features automated follow-ups and simple email templates. Users like its intuitive layout and helpful support team. However, reporting tools are basic and may not satisfy data-heavy teams. The mobile app could also benefit from additional features. It’s a solid choice for businesses that prioritize email campaigns and client organization. For more complex workflows, it may fall short.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

What are the best Copper CRM alternatives for small businesses?

Some of the best CRM platforms for small businesses include tools with simple interfaces, strong automation, and better pricing than Copper. Look for options that support both online and offline access, offer mobile apps for Mac, Android, and Windows, and integrate seamlessly with your existing software.

Are there any free CRM tools similar to Copper?

Yes, some CRM systems offer free plans with basic features, making them a great replacement for Copper if you're on a budget. These free programs usually include contact management, pipeline tracking, and integrations with other business applications. However, advanced features like automation and reporting may require an upgrade.

Which CRM software is better than Copper for client management?

If you need a more flexible CRM with better customer support and customization, look for platforms that focus on client relationship tools, automation, and integrations with business technologies. Many Copper competitors offer a client portal, advanced analytics, and better pricing for growing companies.

# Top 17hats Alternatives for Business Management in 2026

17hats Alternatives for Business Management: What You Need to Know
==================================================================

![Leah Brooks](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35999/leah_brooks-1.png)

Leah Brooks

Writer

February 20, 2025

10 min read

Table of contents

*   [What Is 17hats?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/17hats/#title_0)
    
*   [Limitations of 17hats](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/17hats/#title_1)
    
*   [How to Choose the Best Alternative to 17hats](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/17hats/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![17hats Alternatives for Business Management: What You Need to Know](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35981/17hats.776x0.webp)

Tired of 17hats? Discover fresh alternatives that could better streamline your business and project management, tailored to your unique needs.

If you're a business owner or project manager, you know how crucial it is to find the right tools to keep things running smoothly. 17hats has long been a favorite for managing tasks, client communication, and workflows, but it’s not the only option out there. Sometimes, you just need something a little different or something that suits your needs better. So, let’s talk about 17hats, its limitations, and what alternatives might be a better fit for you.

What Is 17hats?
---------------

17hats is a cloud-based business management software that helps freelancers and small businesses with tasks like client management, invoicing, project tracking, and time management. It's basically like a Swiss Army knife for running a business—pretty handy, right? It has a lot of features, like automating workflows, tracking emails, and integrating with other tools. But even the best tools aren’t perfect, so let’s take a look at what some users think about it.

Limitations of 17hats
---------------------

I’ve checked out what real users say on sites like Capterra, G2, and Trustpilot, and, while 17hats gets plenty of praise, it does have some drawbacks.

1.  **Limited customization options**: Some users feel like 17hats is a bit rigid when it comes to customizing workflows or reports. It’s great if you need basic templates, but if you're looking for something that adapts to your exact business needs, it might fall short.
2.  **Not the best for large teams**: 17hats is designed primarily for solo entrepreneurs and small teams. If your team grows or your needs become more complex, you might find it hard to scale with 17hats. Some users have mentioned the interface gets cluttered and overwhelming as you add more people.
3.  **Lack of advanced project management features**: While 17hats covers the basics of task management, it’s not built to handle more detailed project management, like task dependencies or Gantt charts. If your projects are more complex, you might need something that offers deeper project-tracking functionality.
4.  **Customer support complaints**: A few users on Trustpilot and G2 mentioned slow or unhelpful customer support. That’s definitely a dealbreaker for me—if I’m stuck, I need help fast.

But, don't just take my word for it. Everyone’s got different needs, and for some people, these limitations might not be a big deal.

How to Choose the Best Alternative to 17hats
--------------------------------------------

Choosing the right alternative to 17hats comes down to figuring out what’s most important for your business. Are you a solo entrepreneur? A small team? Or are you looking for something that scales with your growing company? Here are a few things to consider before you make the switch:

1.  **What features do you actually need?** Some people just need [invoicing](https://www.flowlu.com/features/invoice/)
     and time tracking. If that’s you, don’t overthink it—go for something simple. But, if you need [project management](https://www.flowlu.com/features/projects/)
     features like task assignment, timelines, and integrations with other tools, you’ll need something more advanced.
2.  **How customizable does it need to be?** If you need a tool that can mold to your exact needs, you’ll probably want an alternative with more customization options. But if you’re fine with using templates and sticking to a defined workflow, then a simpler tool might be just fine.
3.  **What’s your budget?** Price is always a factor. 17hats is fairly affordable, but if you’re looking for more advanced features, some alternatives may cost a bit more. However, a higher price could be worth it if you’re getting features that actually help your business run smoother.

Best 17hats Competitors and Alternatives in 2025

# Best Agile CRM Alternatives: Top CRM Replacements in 2026

Best Agile CRM Alternatives for Businesses Looking for a Smarter CRM
====================================================================

![Emma Mitchell](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35415/emma_mitchell-1.png)

Emma Mitchell

Writer

February 7, 2025

10 min read

Table of contents

*   [What is Agile CRM?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/agilecrm/#title_0)
    
*   [Where Agile CRM Falls Short](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/agilecrm/#title_1)
    
*   [How to Pick the Best Alternative](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/agilecrm/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Best Agile CRM Alternatives for Businesses Looking for a Smarter CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35397/agilecrm.776x0.webp)

Choosing a CRM isn’t just about ticking off a features checklist. It is about finding a tool that makes your work easier and not more frustrating. If you’re reading this, chances are Agile CRM hasn’t quite lived up to your expectations.

Maybe this software solution is a bit sluggish for you, maybe their support is a headache, or maybe Agile CRM is just not keeping up with your business needs.

Whatever the reason you’re searching for an Agile CRM replacement, you’re not alone in this. Small businesses often start with Agile CRM and gradually they might realize it’s not the best long-term fit. So before jumping to another CRM tool, it helps to understand what Agile CRM does well, where it falls short, and how to pick a smarter alternative for your sales and lead management.

What is Agile CRM?
------------------

Agile CRM is an all-in-one customer relationship management system that combines sales, marketing, and customer service tools in a single platform. The idea? Streamline business operations without juggling multiple software subscriptions. It offers features like contact management, email automation, landing page builders, and even a helpdesk function.

On paper, it sounds great, especially for small businesses looking for an inexpensive CRM with a wide variety of [client management tools](https://www.flowlu.com/features/crm/)
[client management tools](https://www.flowlu.com/features/crm/)
. But in practice, things aren’t always as simple as they seem.

Where Agile CRM Falls Short
---------------------------

User reviews on sites like Capterra, G2, Crozdesk, and Trustpilot reveal some consistent issues. Here’s where Agile CRM tends to frustrate its users:

*   **Slow Performance & Bugs** – If your CRM lags, crashes, or takes forever to load, it slows down your entire sales and project team. And unfortunately, many clients complain about exactly that when using Agile CRM. Large contact lists and complex automations seem to make the system extremely slow.
*   **Customer Support Problems** – Good support can make or break your experience with a CRM. But users frequently report slow responses, unhelpful answers, and even cases of support ignoring their tickets altogether.
*   **Limited Features & Customization** – While Agile CRM offers a lot, some of its tools feel half-baked. For example, its project management features aren’t as strong as dedicated PM tools like Wrike, Flowlu or Monday, and its reporting options lack depth. Many businesses outgrow its capabilities sooner than expected.
*   **Complicated Pricing & Free Tier Limitations** – Agile CRM’s free version looks attractive at first, but once your business starts growing, you quickly run into limits that force an upgrade. And even the paid tiers don’t always justify their cost, given the system’s shortcomings.

These might not be dealbreakers for every small or medium business, but if any of these pain points sound familiar, it’s probably time to start looking at alternatives.

How to Pick the Best Alternative
--------------------------------

Switching CRMs isn’t something you want to do twice. So before making a move, ask yourself:

*   **What features actually matter to my business?** Do you need [better automation](https://www.flowlu.com/help/articles/322-532--automation/)
    ? Stronger reporting? A built-in phone system? Start by listing your must-haves.
*   **Is it easy to use?** The last thing you need is a CRM that makes your team miserable. Look for a system with a clean UX and UI and solid onboarding support.
*   **Can it grow with my business?** Your CRM should be a long-term investment. Make sure it can handle more users, data, and advanced features as your business scales.
*   **Will I get the support I need?** If customer service has been an issue with Agile CRM, don’t make the same mistake twice. Look for providers with consistently strong user feedback on support.

A CRM should help you close more deals and opportunities, not create more headaches. Whether you need something simpler, faster, or just a better supported tool, there are plenty of sales management options out there. The key is finding the right one before you get stuck in another management system that doesn't quite work for your company.

Top Agile CRM Alternatives & Competitors in 2025
------------------------------------------------

![Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34119/flowlu.png)

Flowlu

CRM That Connects Everything

[Try For Free](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

Flowlu is an all-in-one platform that brings together CRM, project management, client portal, knowledge base, and financial tracking tools. The CRM includes features for sales process automation and gives businesses a clear view of their customer journey. Multiple sales pipelines and custom fields make it adaptable for tracking leads and opportunities. It supports contact management, email and telephony integration, and automated workflows. Built-in web forms allow you to capture leads effortlessly. Customizable dashboards and detailed reports help you monitor performance and stay organized. Flowlu integrates with numerous third-party apps, allowing you to integrate it with your existing tools effortlessly. The platform is recognized for its cost-effectiveness, making it an attractive option for small and medium-sized businesses. Its feature set and pricing provide a practical solution for teams seeking a reliable client management tool.

![Zoho CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34120/zoho.png)

Zoho CRM

AI-Powered Client Connections

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/zoho-crm/)

Zoho CRM helps you stay connected with your clients by using AI to automate your CRM processes. It has tools for managing deals, contacts, and workflows. You can communicate with prospects directly through email, phone, or social media. Beyond the basics, it offers advanced features like business intelligence and sales forecasting. However, some users find it has a steep learning curve, higher costs, and slower support.

![Hubspot](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34121/hubspot.png)

Hubspot

Real-Time Customer Insights

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/hubspot/)

HubSpot is a well-known CRM that lets you track sales activity in real time. It’s great for building a central spot to store all your customer info. You get email tracking, handy templates, and integrations with Gmail and Outlook. Also, you can create custom dashboards, dig into analytics, and handle email campaigns like a pro. It’s got forms for lead capturing, plus segmentation tools and even chatbots to keep things moving. For solo entrepreneurs and small businesses, the cost can really sting. So, while it’s got bells and whistles, it might not fit every budget.

![SuiteDash](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34122/suitedash.png)

SuiteDash

Centralized Sales & Service

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/suitedash/)

SuiteDash calls itself a client portal software but also includes a CRM with tools for managing sales funnels. It helps you keep all client information in one place and offers automation features like scheduling follow-ups and creating new contacts automatically. You can choose from four different styles to monitor pipelines from various aspects. It also provides client onboarding tools like forms, checklists, and proposals. However, users often mention the platform feels slow and overwhelming during setup due to the many modules and unclear connections between them.

![Freshsales](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34123/freshsales.png)

Freshsales

Simplified AI-Driven Selling

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/)

Freshsales is an AI-powered CRM to make selling easier. Its Freddy AI helps you run targeted campaigns, qualify leads with intent scores, and gives you useful recommendations. The platform is all about automation to reduce manual tasks and features a simple interface. Freshsales provides customizable sales reports, and tools to create outreach campaigns. It integrates with email, messaging apps, SMS, Zoom, chat, and phone systems. There's also a marketplace for connecting with hundreds of popular apps. However, many users find the reporting and analysis tools overly complex, which can be a significant drawback.

![Vtiger](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34124/vtiger_2x.png)

Vtiger

Advanced CRM Made Simple

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/vtiger/)

Vtiger is a user-friendly CRM that connects with over 500 apps. It includes advanced tools for lead routing and prospect segmentation. The mobile app is especially handy, supporting voice notes and business card scanning. Vtiger also offers email marketing features, automated email sequences, and integrations with phone, chat, SMS, and websites. It helps you create forecast scenarios and predict estimated sales revenue. Still, many users mention that customer support could be improved.

![BaseCRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34125/basecrm_2x.png)

BaseCRM

Effortless Sales Simplification

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/basecrm/)

BaseCRM, now Zendesk Sell, focuses on simplifying sales tracking. It helps organize leads, track deals and activities, and automate repetitive tasks. You can also set up automation rules and triggers to streamline client journeys. Email tools and mobile access make it useful for teams on the move. Users appreciate its clean interface and reporting options. Still, some customers occasionally complain about slow customer service responses.

![SugarCRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34126/sugarcrm.jpg)

SugarCRM

Custom Dashboards, Seamless Automation

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/sugarcrm/)

SugarCRM offers tools for sale and marketing management, and customer support. This software is very adaptable and lets you create dashboards and reports that match your company’s needs. Automation features save time on routine tasks. SugarCRM integrates well with accounting tools, messengers, ERP and so on. While it offers plenty of features, some users find the interface tricky and the learning curve steep.

![Streak](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34128/streak_2x.png)

Streak

CRM Inside Your Inbox

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/streak/)

Streak works directly in Gmail, so there’s no need to switch between platforms. It helps you manage contacts and track sales within your inbox. The tool supports email tracking, templates, and integrations with Google apps. It’s great for keeping things simple. It has filters, sorting and grouping options to keep your data clean. That said, users report that the mobile version lacks some desktop features.

Copper

Google Workspace CRM Partner

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/copper/)

Copper works within Google Workspace, letting you manage sales and leads right from Gmail. It simplifies tasks like contact tracking and pipeline updates. Many users like its clean, intuitive design. You can also set up the workflow automation, so Copper will do the tasks for you. However, its reporting tools can feel basic. Some also mention limitations in the mobile app.

![Nimble](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34130/nimble.png)

Nimble

Social Media-Enhanced CRM

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/nimble/)

Nimble merges CRM with social media. You can track contacts, monitor conversations, and manage email outreach all in one place. It stands out for its ability to connect with various platforms. Nimble also offers ready-made templates with pre-defined fields and stages for your workflows. That said, reporting options are fairly minimal. This can be a drawback for users who need deeper insights.

![Nutshell](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34131/nutshell.png)

Nutshell

CRM for Growing Teams

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/nutshell/)

Nutshell caters to small businesses with features for email integration, sales automation, and team collaboration. The interface is straightforward, and the pricing fits tighter budgets. Nutshell provides native integrations, connections through 3rd-party apps and AppConnect. On the downside, its customization options are limited. Larger teams might outgrow it quickly.

![Salesforce](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34132/salesforce.png)

Salesforce

Scalable Power for Teams

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/salesforce/)

Salesforce is packed with features for managing sales, customer support, and marketing. It’s ideal for big teams needing scalable tools. Salesforce is integrated with AI, which means it can write emails for you, generate summaries, or provide real-time predictions, among other options. But all those features come at a price. Many users also mention the platform feels overwhelming without proper training.

![Insightly](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34133/insightly.png)

Insightly

CRM Meets Project Management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/)

Insightly combines CRM and project management. It’s handy for keeping track of both customers and tasks in one system. Insightly includes business process automation to free your team from routine tasks. You can also create real-time reports to visualize your sales data. The integration options are useful, but the interface feels clunky to some. Reporting tools, while functional, are less detailed than what many users expect.

![Pipedrive](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34134/pipedrive.png)

Pipedrive

Visual Pipelines, Simplified Sales

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/pipedrive/)

Pipedrive focuses on keeping sales organized. It uses a clean, visual pipeline to track deals and activities. You can automate tasks like follow-ups and reminders, and save time and efforts for your team. Email integration makes communication easy. Pipedrive also offers automated sales conversations with AI-powered prompts and insights. Users like its simplicity and find it easy to pick up. However, reporting features feel basic to some. Perhaps, Pipedrive could benefit if they implement more in-depth analytics for their clients.

![Bitrix24](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34135/bitrix24.png)

Bitrix24

Collaboration Meets CRM

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/bitrix24/)

Bitrix24 offers a versatile mix of CRM, communication, and project management tools. It lets teams collaborate through video calls, email tracking, and shared workspaces. Automated notifications via SMS or email can be set up to keep clients informed without manual effort. An AI assistant is included to help generate marketing content and brainstorm ideas, saving time for your team. Many users appreciate the wide range of tools available. It’s affordable, especially for smaller teams. On the downside, the interface can be overwhelming. Slow system performance is another common complaint.

![Kommo](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34136/kommo_2x.png)

Kommo focuses on messaging-based sales. It connects directly with apps like WhatsApp, Messenger, and Telegram. This makes real-time communication with leads simple. You can automate follow-ups and track deals easily. Kommo’s AI features help you to create summaries of your conversations or suggest replies. The interface is clean, and most features are intuitive. Customization, though, is somewhat limited. Support response times could also be better.

![EngageBay](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34137/engagebay_2x.png)

Affordable All-In-One CRM

EngageBay is a great option for small businesses. It incorporates marketing, sales, and service tools into one platform. EngageBay’s main features include email campaigns, lead generation, and a helpdesk. You can integrate SMS, track opens and link clicks. The free plan makes it appealing to startups. Many users like its range of options. Some, however, report bugs in the system. The learning curve can also be steep for beginners.

![Less Annoying CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34138/less_annoying_crm_2x.png)

Less Annoying CRM

Simplified CRM for SMBs

Less Annoying CRM keeps things simple. It’s built for small businesses that need basic tools. Contact and task management are easy to set up. The interface is clean, with no unnecessary extras. It’s affordable too, which users love. It integrates well with calendar apps, email, invoicing software, VOIP, web forms, video communication tools and other software you may utilize. That said, it lacks advanced features. There’s also no dedicated app for mobile use.

![monday Sales CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34139/monday_sales_crm_2x.png)

monday Sales CRM

Customizable Sales Tracking

monday Sales CRM offers flexibility for tracking deals and managing contacts. Built on the monday.com platform, it integrates easily with other tools. Automation options help reduce repetitive tasks. Teams enjoy the drag-and-drop customization. Its AI features let you generate tasks, compose emails, summarize calls and so much more. However, the setup process can take time. For smaller businesses, the price might feel high. Some users also find it overly reliant on custom workflows.

![NetHunt](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34140/nethunt_2x.png)

NetHunt

CRM Inside Gmail Workflow

NetHunt turns Gmail into a CRM. It allows you to manage deals and automate workflows without leaving your inbox. Email tracking and follow-ups are straightforward. It’s great for teams already using Google Workspace. NetHunt also includes reminders, calendar sync, and mentions for team collaboration. Many users praise its seamless integration. On mobile, though, certain features are missing. Larger teams may also feel it lacks advanced reporting.

![Pipeline](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34141/pipeline_2x.png)

Pipeline

Organized Sales Made Easy

Pipeline CRM simplifies sales processes. It tracks deals, contacts, and tasks in one place. Reporting tools offer basic insights into team performance. The interface is user-friendly and doesn’t overwhelm. Pipeline CRm automates workflows, sends reminders to your colleagues and suggests next steps. Customer service is quick and helpful, which users appreciate. Still, customization is limited. Reports could also use more depth for detailed analysis.

![Salesflare](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34142/salesflare_2x.png)

Salesflare

Automated CRM for SMBs

Salesflare is a CRM specialized in automating data entry for SMBs. It tracks emails, calls, and meetings automatically. The system also pulls contact details from online sources, saving users time. Many appreciate its simple setup and clean interface. Email tracking and pipeline management are popular features. However, customization options are limited. Some users feel the mobile app lacks important features found in the desktop version. For smaller teams, the software is a great fit and meets most needs. Larger companies, however, may find it too limiting for more complex workflows.

CRM with Process Automation

Creatio blends CRM tools with process automation in one platform. It simplifies lead management, tracks sales, and handles workflows. The system adapts to fit various business needs. Users praise its flexibility. Teams can build unique solutions quickly. However, setup can be overwhelming due to its many options. New users often report needing additional training to get started. Once configured, it’s reliable for streamlining operations. Advanced reporting tools make it appealing to data-driven teams. On the downside, customer support response times could improve.

Close

Unified Communication Hub

Close brings email, SMS, and calls together in one place. It simplifies how teams connect with prospects and leads. A single inbox keeps all conversations organized. Close’s automation tools help with follow-ups and reminders. Reporting tools offer insights into team performance. Users find it straightforward to set up and use. However, some find the pricing steep for smaller teams. Customization is another area where it falls short. For teams that prioritize communication, it’s a solid choice.

![Capsule](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34145/capsule_2x.png)

Essentials for Small Business

Capsule CRM focuses on simplicity and small business needs. It organizes contacts, tracks sales, and manages tasks in one place. Users like its straightforward design and affordable pricing. It integrates well with tools like Xero and Mailchimp. However, advanced features like workflow automation are missing. Reporting tools are basic but sufficient for smaller teams. The mobile app works well but could include more features. It’s a good option for businesses needing the essentials without the clutter.

![Breakcold](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34146/breakcold_2x.png)

Breakcold

CRM Meets Social Selling

Breakcold combines CRM functions with social selling tools. It tracks interactions on LinkedIn, Twitter, and email in one platform. Users can engage prospects directly from the dashboard. It’s great for teams focused on relationship-building. However, traditional CRM features like deal tracking and task automation are limited. The interface can also feel clunky to new users. Social media-driven teams appreciate its niche approach. For those needing broader functionality, it might not be the best fit.

![Folk](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34147/folk_2x.png)

Folk

Collaborative CRM for Teams

Folk is a modern CRM that emphasizes collaboration and simplicity. It lets teams manage contacts, assign tasks, and track interactions in one place. The interface is minimalistic and easy to navigate. It’s especially useful for small teams managing shared responsibilities. However, it lacks some advanced features seen in older CRMs. Integration options are still growing, which may limit its appeal. Bugs are occasionally reported by users. It works best for startups and smaller teams.

![CRMOne](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34148/crmone_2x.png)

CRMOne

Streamline Customer Interactions

CRMOne provides basic tools for contact management, sales tracking, and marketing automation. It’s designed to cover the essentials without overwhelming users. Many appreciate its straightforward interface. However, reviews suggest that the features are limited compared to competitors. Advanced analytics and integration options are lacking. Some users feel it’s best suited for teams with very simple needs. It’s an option for those who value simplicity over depth.

![BigContacts](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/34149/bigcontacts_2x.png)

BigContacts

Contact Management Simplified

BigContacts focuses on contact management and email marketing for small to medium businesses. It features automated follow-ups and simple email templates. Users like its intuitive layout and helpful support team. However, reporting tools are basic and may not satisfy data-heavy teams. The mobile app could also benefit from additional features. It’s a solid choice for businesses that prioritize email campaigns and client organization. For more complex workflows, it may fall short.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

What are the best software programs like Agile CRM for businesses?

There are plenty of CRM platforms similar to Agile CRM, offering a mix of automation, client management, and marketing tools. The best choice depends on whether you need a simple CRM for small teams or a more advanced system for a big company.

Are there any free Agile CRM competitors for small businesses?

Yes, several CRM applications provide free plans with core features like contact management and basic automation. However, most free options have limits on users, storage, or features, so it’s important to check what’s included before committing.

What is a good replacement for Agile CRM that works on Mac, Windows, and mobile platforms?

If you need a CRM that runs on multiple platforms—including Mac, Windows, Android, and iOS—look for cloud-based CRM tools with strong mobile apps and offline capabilities. Some CRMs also offer desktop apps for businesses that prefer a local software option.

# Best Daylite Alternative | Top CRM Options for Mac & More

Best Daylite Alternative: Find a CRM That Works for Your Business
=================================================================

![Ethan Kim](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35347/ethan_kim-1.png)

Ethan Kim

Content Specialist

February 6, 2025

9 min read

Table of contents

*   [What Is Daylite CRM?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/daylite/#title_0)
    
*   [Where Daylite Falls Short](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/daylite/#title_1)
    
*   [How to Choose the Best Daylite Alternative](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/daylite/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Best Daylite Alternative: Find a CRM That Works for Your Business](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35328/daylite.776x0.webp)

If you’ve been using Daylite CRM, you might be wondering if it is still the right fit for your business. Maybe your company already outgrown it, or maybe it’s just not working the way you need.

Either way, to find a better CRM solution, you need to understand what Daylite does well and what it lacks.

What Is Daylite CRM?
--------------------

Daylite is a Mac-only CRM built for small businesses that want to manage contacts, projects, and sales in one place. This software is especially popular with service-based businesses like marketing agencies, business consultants, and real estate teams. Since it’s designed for Apple users, it integrates well with macOS and iOS apps like Mail and Calendar.

Where Daylite Falls Short
-------------------------

Daylite has some useful features, but real user reviews highlight a few common issues:

*   **Mac-Only Restriction** – If your team uses Windows or Android devices, they’re out of luck. This makes collaboration harder for mixed-device teams.
*   **Outdated Interface** – Many users feel the design looks dated and isn’t as intuitive as modern CRMs. Some features feel clunky or take too many clicks.
*   **Limited Integrations** – While it works well within the Apple ecosystem, connecting it to other business tools can be a hassle. Users often mention wanting more automation and flexibility.
*   **Sync Issues** – Some Dylite customers report delays or errors when syncing data across devices. This can be frustrating when you need real-time updates.
*   **Pricing vs. Value** – While this is not the most expensive CRM out there, some customers still feel the price doesn’t match the features they get (especially with the lack of cloud-based collaboration options for their teams).

How to Choose the Best Daylite Alternative
------------------------------------------

If Daylite isn’t working for you, here’s what to look for in a better customer management system:

*   **Cross-Platform Access** – Make sure your new CRM works on both Mac and Windows (and ideally has a [mobile app for both iOS and Android](https://www.flowlu.com/mobile/)
    ).
*   **User-Friendly Design** – A clean and friendly interface makes all the difference. You shouldn’t need a manual to figure things out on how to manage your leads and sales.
*   **Strong Integrations** – Your CRM should connect with the tools you already use, like email, calendars, accounting and project management apps.
*   **Reliable Cloud Syncing** – No one wants to deal with missing or outdated data. A cloud-based system should sync automatically without issues.
*   **Fair Pricing** – Look for a [CRM tool](https://www.flowlu.com/features/crm/)
     that offers good value for the features you actually need—without hidden costs or overpriced add-ons many tools offer today.

If you’re switching from Daylite, the best CRM for you will depend on your current workflow, team size, and what features matter most for your company. The good news? There are plenty of great options that give you more flexibility without locking you into Apple-only tools.
