# Top Kissflow Alternatives for Better Workflow Management

Best Alternatives to Kissflow for Workflow Management
=====================================================

![Ethan Kim](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35845/ethan_kim-1.png)

Ethan Kim

Content Specialist

February 17, 2025

9 min read

Table of contents

*   [What is Kissflow?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/kissflow/#title_0)
    
*   [Why Do People Look for Kissflow Alternatives?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/kissflow/#title_1)
    
*   [What to Look for in a Kissflow Alternative](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/kissflow/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Best Alternatives to Kissflow for Workflow Management](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35827/kissflow.776x0.webp)

Thinking about switching from Kissflow? If you're looking for something smoother, more customizable, and better supported, we break down the best alternatives—based on real user feedback—to help you find the right fit.

Managing workflows shouldn't feel like a never-ending puzzle. You need a tool that simplifies things, not one that adds more headaches. Kissflow is a well-known workflow management tool, but is it the best fit for you and your business? Maybe, maybe not. Let’s take a real look at what Kissflow does, where it falls short (based on actual client feedback), and what to keep in mind when picking an alternative software for your team.

What is Kissflow?
-----------------

Kissflow is a cloud-based workflow automation platform that helps businesses manage processes, cases, and projects. The idea? Make it easy for teams to [build workflows](https://www.flowlu.com/blog/flowlu-tips/business-process-automation/)
 without needing a developer. In theory, that sounds great. In practice? Well, let’s just say not everyone is thrilled.

Why Do People Look for Kissflow Alternatives?
---------------------------------------------

Not every tool works for every business. Here are some of the biggest frustrations users have shared on Capterra, G2, Trustpilot, and Crozdesk:

*   **Clunky user experience**. The interface isn’t as intuitive as it could be, and some users say it takes too much time to get the hang of it. Who has time for that?
*   **Limited customization**. Need a super-flexible workflow that fits your exact process? You might run into some roadblocks.
*   **Slow performance**. Some users report lag, especially when handling larger workflows. Nothing kills productivity faster than waiting on a tool that’s supposed to speed things up.
*   **Support that’s hit or miss**. A few users say customer support is slow to respond or not always helpful. If you’re stuck, you don’t want to wait days for an answer.

What to Look for in a Kissflow Alternative
------------------------------------------

So, if Kissflow isn’t quite right, what should you look for in a replacement? A few things matter more than others:

*   **Ease of use**. Does the platform make sense right away, or does it feel like learning a new language?
*   **Customization options**. Can you tweak workflows to fit your exact needs, or are you stuck working around limitations?
*   **Integrations**. Will it connect smoothly with the [tools you already use](https://www.flowlu.com/integrations/)
    , or will you spend hours setting up workarounds?
*   **Speed and reliability**. Does it keep up with your work, or will you be staring at a loading screen half the day?
*   **Customer support**. When things go wrong (because they always do at some point), will you get fast and useful help?

No tool is perfect, but some get much closer than others. And if you’re looking for something better than Kissflow, there are plenty of solid options out there. The trick is finding one that fits your business like a glove.

Best Kissflow Competitors & Alternatives for Automating Business Workflows
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Flowlu offers multiple task views—like Kanban, list, or calendar—so you can visualize work the way that suits your team best. With all your data in one place, making informed decisions is simpler than ever. It's all about reducing the effort of daily tasks, so you can focus on what really matters—growing your business and delivering value to your clients.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

Is Flowlu a good alternative to Kissflow?

Yes, Flowlu is a strong alternative to Kissflow, especially if you're seeking an all-in-one business management solution. While Kissflow focuses primarily on workflow automation, Flowlu offers a broader range of features, including project management, CRM, financial management, and collaboration tools. Users have praised Flowlu for its comprehensive approach, though it may have a slightly steeper learning curve due to its extensive functionalities.

What are some top alternatives to Kissflow for workflow management?

Several platforms offer robust workflow management capabilities comparable to or surpassing Kissflow. Notable alternatives include:

*   **Flowlu**: An all-in-one productivity platform that provides customizable views, integrations with numerous apps, and a user-friendly interface.
*   **FlowForma**: A no-code tool praised for its ease of use and strong support, often scoring higher than Kissflow in user satisfaction.
*   **Appian**: A low-code application platform suitable for enterprises seeking extensive process automation and integration capabilities.

Each of these platforms offers unique features, so it's essential to assess your specific business needs when selecting a tool.

What factors should I consider when choosing a workflow management tool?

Selecting the right workflow management tool involves evaluating several key aspects:

*   **Ease of Use**: Is the platform intuitive? Can your team adopt it without extensive training?
*   **Customization**: Does it allow tailoring workflows to fit your specific processes?
*   **Integration Capabilities**: Can it seamlessly connect with your existing tools and systems?
*   **Scalability**: Will the tool accommodate your business growth and evolving needs?
*   **Support and Resources**: Is there reliable customer support and accessible learning materials?

# Best Alternatives to Jira for 2026 for Project Management

Best Jira Alternatives: Top Tools for Project Management
========================================================

![Kristen Chung](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32392/kristen-flw-1.png)

Kristen Chung

Writer

January 13, 2025

9 min read

Table of contents

*   [Drawbacks of Jira](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/jira/#title_0)
    
*   [What to Look for in Jira Alternatives](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/jira/#title_1)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Best Jira Alternatives: Top Tools for Project Management](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32374/jira.776x0.webp)

Jira is a well-known project management tool, especially popular with software development teams. Its tracking features and customization options make it great for managing complex projects.

But sometimes, Jira’s many features can feel overwhelming, particularly for teams looking for something simpler. If you’re searching for a free alternative, something easier for your team to learn, or a more flexible option, there are plenty of tools that offer similar functionality without the complexity.

Drawbacks of Jira
-----------------

Even though Jira is a popular choice, it has a few downsides that might have you considering other options:

### Too Complicated for Non-Tech Teams

Jira was designed for tech teams, so it can feel cluttered or overly complex for those in non-technical roles. If your team needs something more straightforward, Jira’s features might get in the way instead of helping.

### Lack of Simplicity

Jira is great for complex workflows, but this can be a problem for teams who just need basic task management. If you’re working on projects outside of software development, Jira might be more than you need—especially if you want a tool that’s easy to use and navigate.

### Costs Can Add Up

While Jira has a free plan, it’s limited. As your team grows or needs more advanced features, the price can quickly rise. For small teams or startups on a budget, this might be hard to justify. Looking for alternatives with better pricing options can be a smarter move.

### Performance Issues

For teams handling multiple projects, Jira can sometimes be slow. This can lead to frustration if you need to switch between tasks quickly and the software isn’t as responsive as you’d like.

What to Look for in Jira Alternatives
-------------------------------------

If you’re thinking about switching from Jira, look for a tool that fixes these problems while still offering the features you need:

### Easy to Use

Jira can feel complicated, so an alternative should be much simpler. Look for a tool that’s easy for your team to get started with, even if they aren’t in software development. Monday.com or ClickUp are known for their clean, intuitive design.

### Flexibility for Different Workflows

While Jira works well for software development, teams in other industries might need more flexible tools. Look for something that can handle different workflows, whether you’re in marketing, design, or product management.

### Affordable Pricing

If Jira’s pricing feels too steep, look for a tool that offers a free or more affordable version that grows with your team. Flowlu, for example, has a free tier and transparent pricing that won’t surprise you as your needs grow.

### Fast and Lightweight

If Jira feels slow or bulky, consider switching to something faster and more lightweight. You want a tool that responds quickly and doesn’t slow your team down with unnecessary features.

### Good for Collaboration

Collaboration is key for successful projects. The best Jira alternatives will make it easy for your team to communicate and work together. Trello, for example, offers real-time chat, easy task assignment, and file sharing, all in a simple interface that keeps everyone on the same page.

Top Alternatives to Jira Software for Project Management
--------------------------------------------------------

Flowlu offers multiple task views—like Kanban, list, or calendar—so you can visualize work the way that suits your team best. With all your data in one place, making informed decisions is simpler than ever. It's all about reducing the effort of daily tasks, so you can focus on what really matters—growing your business and delivering value to your clients.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

What are the best Jira alternatives for my business?

If you're looking for a replacement for Jira, there are several strong alternatives available. Tools like Monday.com, Flowlu, and Asana offer flexible, user-friendly project management features. These platforms are great for tracking tasks, streamlining collaboration, and making it easier to manage projects, without the complexity that often comes with Jira.

Is there a free alternative to Jira?

Yes, many platforms provide free versions that are excellent alternatives to Jira. For example, Trello and Flowlu offer robust free plans that allow you to manage projects efficiently without any cost. These options are ideal for small teams or startups that need effective project management tools on a limited budget.

Can I find a Jira alternative that works on Mac, Windows, and Android?

While there isn’t an exact copy of Jira, tools like Flowlu, ClickUp, and Monday.com are available across multiple platforms, including Mac, Windows, and Android. These tools offer similar functionality, so your team can stay connected and work efficiently, no matter the device they’re using.

Are there any offline alternatives to Jira?

Most project management tools, including Jira, are built to function online. However, some options, like Notion, allow you to work offline, which can be helpful for teams that need flexibility when internet access is limited. With Notion, you can continue managing tasks and projects even without a stable connection.

What should I consider when choosing a Jira alternative?

When picking a replacement for Jira, it's important to choose a tool that fits your business’s needs without overwhelming you. Look for a solution that has a clean interface, flexible customization options, and integrates smoothly with other apps your team uses. Tools like Flowlu, Monday.com, and Asana can offer the right balance between ease of use and advanced project management features.

# Best Kantata Alternatives: Affordable Project Management

The Best Kantata (Mavenlink) Alternatives for Agencies
======================================================

![Ingrid Johansen](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35953/ingrid_johansen-1.jpg)

Ingrid Johansen

Copywriter

February 20, 2025

11 min read

Table of contents

*   [What is Kantata?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/kantata-mavenlink/#title_0)
    
*   [The Limitations of Kantata (Based on Real User Feedback)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/kantata-mavenlink/#title_1)
    
*   [How to Choose the Best Alternative to Kantata](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/kantata-mavenlink/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![The Best Kantata (Mavenlink) Alternatives for Agencies](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35935/mavenlink.776x0.webp)

Not sure if Kantata (formerly Mavenlink) is the right choice for your small business? Explore the limitations of Kantata and discover affordable, user-friendly alternatives that won’t break the bank or require endless training.

Alright, so you're considering Kantata (formerly Mavenlink) for your business. I get it. It's a popular tool, especially for project managers in larger companies. But if you’re a small business, is it really the right fit for you? Or is there a better, more affordable option out there? Let’s break it down without all the fluff.

What is Kantata?
----------------

Kantata (formerly Mavelink) is an all-in-one project management platform that helps businesses with project tracking, collaboration, resource management, and financials. It’s built to handle everything from simple tasks to complex workflows—making it great for larger organizations with lots of moving parts. But here’s the catch: It might not be the best choice for small businesses.

Now, don’t get me wrong. It has solid features, and if you’re running a large operation with a big budget, it might work perfectly for you. But is it really worth the steep price tag and complexity for a small team? Probably not.

The Limitations of Kantata (Based on Real User Feedback)
--------------------------------------------------------

Before you get sold on the idea of using Kantata, let’s talk about some of the issues that users have raised. I did a bit of digging on platforms like Capterra, G2, Trustpilot, and Crodesk, and here's what I found.

1.  **Price** – If you’ve been doing your homework on project management tools, you’ve probably noticed the same thing I did: Kantata is expensive. Some users say that while it offers a ton of features, you end up paying for a lot of things you don’t need. For small businesses with tighter budgets, that can be a tough pill to swallow.
2.  **Steep Learning Curve** – This one is a biggie. A lot of users mention how complicated Kantata can be. Some even joke that it feels like you need a degree to figure out how to use it. Not exactly ideal when you’re juggling a million things as a small business owner or manager. Time is money, and you really don’t want to waste it trying to understand a new platform.
3.  **Customization Overload** – On paper, having a customizable platform is great, but it can also be overwhelming. While Kantata offers a lot of flexibility, it’s been noted that setting things up the way you want takes forever. Small business owners who are looking for something simple and easy to use often find this to be frustrating.
4.  **Customer Support** – When you run into issues (and we all do), you want to know that customer support has your back. Unfortunately, some users have reported slow response times and unresolved issues. As a small business, waiting weeks for support to fix something could seriously set you back.

So, does Kantata work? Sure. Is it the best fit for a small business? Probably not.

How to Choose the Best Alternative to Kantata
---------------------------------------------

If you’re not sold on Kantata, don’t worry. There are plenty of other project management tools out there that might be a better fit for your business. But how do you know which one to pick? Here are a few things I’d recommend considering when you start looking around.

1.  **Define Your Needs** – Before you start exploring alternatives, take a moment to think about exactly what you need. Are you looking for [project management](https://www.flowlu.com/features/projects/)
    , task tracking, team collaboration? Maybe just basic time tracking? A simple tool could do the job without the bells and whistles that Kantata comes with.
2.  **Look for Simplicity** – Small businesses don’t need a platform that requires endless hours of training. Look for something that’s easy to use and intuitive. The goal is to get your team up and running without a steep learning curve. Trust me, you don’t need the complexity of a tool designed for large enterprises.
3.  **Affordable Pricing** – Let’s be honest: as a [small business](https://www.flowlu.com/solutions/crm-software-for-small-business/)
    , you’ve got to keep costs low. You don’t need to be paying for features that you’ll never use. Look for software that’s priced reasonably and offers flexibility, so you only pay for what you need.
4.  **Customer Support** – If you’re anything like me, you want to make sure there’s solid support behind the tool you choose. Check out reviews to get a sense of how responsive customer service is. If other users are consistently frustrated with slow response times or unhelpful agents, keep looking.
5.  **Scalability** – You might be small now, but you’ve got plans to grow. So, choose a tool that can grow with you. You don’t want to be stuck with something that can’t handle your needs down the line, or worse, forces you to switch platforms once you start scaling.

Kantata Competitors You Should Consider for Your Small Business
---------------------------------------------------------------

![Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/31510/flowlu_4.png)

Flowlu

Your all-in-one work management solution

Creatio blends CRM tools with process automation in one platform. It simplifies lead management, tracks sales, and handles workflows. The system adapts to fit various business needs. Users praise its flexibility. Teams can build unique solutions quickly. However, setup can be overwhelming due to its many options. New users often report needing additional training to get started. Once configured, it’s reliable for streamlining operations. Advanced reporting tools make it appealing to data-driven teams. On the downside, customer support response times could improve.

NetSuite is an all-in-one business software that handles accounting, inventory, and operations in one system. It’s built for companies that need more than just spreadsheets but don’t want ten different tools that don’t talk to each other. It’s flexible enough for growing businesses and big enough for companies that need serious financial tracking.

It’s a good fit for businesses that want flexibility without a bunch of separate subscriptions.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

Is Flowlu a Good Alternative to Kantata for Small Businesses?

Yes, Flowlu can be a great alternative to Kantata, especially for small businesses. It offers many of the same features, like project management, task tracking, and collaboration, but at a more affordable price point. The user-friendly interface and scalable plans make it a solid choice for growing teams who need flexibility without the complexity and high costs of Kantata.

What Are the Main Benefits of Using a Kantata Alternative?

The biggest benefits of using a Kantata alternative are cost-efficiency, ease of use, and faster implementation. Small businesses often find that they don’t need all the advanced features that Kantata offers, and a simpler, more intuitive tool can help them stay organized and manage projects without the steep learning curve.

How Do I Choose the Right Alternative to Kantata for My Business?

Choosing the right Kantata alternative depends on your specific needs. Consider factors like budget, the size of your team, the complexity of your projects, and the tools you already use. Look for options that offer the features you need (like task management or time tracking) without the unnecessary extras. Make sure the platform is scalable as your business grows and that it provides strong customer support.

# Top Microsoft Project Alternatives | Best Free & Paid Tools

Top Alternatives to Microsoft Project for Better Project Management
===================================================================

![Leah Brooks](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/33175/leah_brooks-1.png)

Leah Brooks

Writer

November 5, 2024

7 min read

Table of contents

*   [Why Microsoft Project Might Not Be the Best Fit](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/microsoft-project/#title_0)
    
*   [What to Consider in a Microsoft Project Competitors](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/microsoft-project/#title_1)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Top Alternatives to Microsoft Project for Better Project Management](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/33157/ms_projects.776x0.webp)

Microsoft Project has attracted many project managers for years. However, it can and doesn’t work for every team. Some find it tricky to learn, too expensive, or not flexible enough.

Fortunately, there are several other software solutions that are easy to use, budget-friendly, and suit different needs. Let’s look at why Microsoft Project might not be ideal PM software for everyone, what to seek in a replacement, and a few top choices.

Why Microsoft Project Might Not Be the Best Fit
-----------------------------------------------

Before we explore alternatives, here are some reasons teams may look elsewhere:

*   **Learning Curve:** Microsoft Project has a lot of features. New users can struggle to get comfortable with it quickly.
*   **Cost:** It’s not cheap. For smaller teams, this can be a dealbreaker, making free or low-cost options more attractive.
*   **Limited Flexibility:** Microsoft Project mostly sticks to traditional workflows, which might not work for teams using agile methods.
*   **Complex Interface:** The layout can feel confusing, especially if your team wants something simpler.

What to Consider in a Microsoft Project Competitors
---------------------------------------------------

When hunting for a replacement, here are a few helpful things to keep in mind:

*   **Easy to Use:** An intuitive setup lets your team start without much training.
*   **Affordable:** Check for tools with free options or pricing that fits your budget, whether you’re a small team or a growing business.
*   **Supports Collaboration:** Look for tools that make teamwork easy. Real-time updates, task sharing, and chat features are a big plus.
*   **Flexible Workflows:** The best tools let you adjust tasks, reports, and workflows to match your style.
*   **Cloud Access:** Many modern tools work online, so you can log in from anywhere—helpful for remote or hybrid teams.

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Best Portfolio Manager (former LiquidPlanner) Alternatives for Your Workflow](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32595/liquidplanner.776x0.webp)

Project management helps teams stay on track, meet deadlines, and align their work with business goals.

Portfolio Manager by Tempo (former LiquidPlanner) is known for its scheduling and resource management features, but it also has limitations. If you're looking for a better fit, here are some alternatives and what to consider when choosing the right one.

Portfolio Manager (former LiquidPlanner) Limitations
----------------------------------------------------

*   **Complexity:** LiquidPlanner can be hard to learn because of its many features.
    
*   **Cost:** Its pricing can be expensive for growing teams or small businesses.
    
*   **Limited Flexibility:** Some users find it doesn’t offer enough options for customizing workflows.
    

What to Look for in Portfolio Manager Alternatives
--------------------------------------------------

*   **Ease of Use:** A tool that’s easy to use with helpful features like drag-and-drop tasks can make your work smoother.
    
*   **Flexible Pricing:** Choose a tool that fits your budget and can grow with your team.
    
*   **Integration:** Make sure the tool connects easily with apps like Slack or Google Drive.
    
*   **Collaboration Tools:** Look for built-in communication features like comments and file sharing within tasks.
    
*   **Automation:** Tools that automate routine tasks, such as updating project statuses, save time.
    

Top Project Management Tools Better Than LiquidPlanner
------------------------------------------------------

With all your data in one place, making informed decisions is simpler than ever. # Best Lucidchart Alternatives for Mind Mapping & Diagramming

The Best Mind Mapping & Diagramming Tools If You’re Over Lucidchart
===================================================================

![Ethan Kim](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36275/ethan_kim-1.png)

Ethan Kim

Content Specialist

February 27, 2025

9 min read

Table of contents

*   [What's Lucidchart All About?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/lucidchart/#title_0)
    
*   [Why Are Some Users Seeking Alternatives?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/lucidchart/#title_1)
    
*   [What Makes a Great Lucidchart Alternative?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/lucidchart/#title_2)
    
*   [How to Choose the Right Alternative](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/lucidchart/#title_3)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![The Best Mind Mapping &amp; Diagramming Tools If You’re Over Lucidchart](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36257/lucidchart.776x0.webp)

Lucidchart isn’t the only option for mind mapping and diagramming—and for some, it’s not even the best one. If you're looking for a smoother, more affordable, or just a better-fitting tool, check out these top Lucidchart alternatives based on real user feedback.

Well, you're on the hunt for the best alternatives to Lucidchart for mind mapping, brainstorming, and diagramming? Maybe you’re tired of clunky performance, or the pricing just isn’t sitting right with you. Or maybe you just want something that feels easier to use—because let’s be honest, nobody wants to spend half their time figuring out how to make a simple flowchart.

Whatever the reason, you’ve got options. And good ones, too. The right tool can make all the difference in how smoothly your ideas flow, whether you're sketching out a new project plan, mapping customer journeys (ugh, I hate that word, but here we are), or just making sense of the mess inside your head.

So, let’s break it down. What makes Lucidchart popular? Why are people looking for something else? And more importantly—what should you be using instead?

What's Lucidchart All About?
----------------------------

Lucidchart is a web-based platform that helps folks like us create flowcharts, diagrams, and mind maps. It's been a go-to for many because of its user-friendly interface and a bunch of templates that make diagramming a breeze.

Why Are Some Users Seeking Alternatives?
----------------------------------------

While Lucidchart has its fans, not everyone is head over heels for it. I took a stroll through some user reviews on sites like Capterra, G2, Crozdesk, TrustRadius, and Trustpilot to get the lowdown. Here's what I found:

*   **Pricing Concerns**: Some users feel that Lucidchart's pricing doesn't quite match up with the features they get. They'd like more bang for their buck.
*   **Learning Curve**: A few folks mentioned that getting the hang of all the features can be a bit tricky. They'd prefer something more intuitive.
*   **Performance Issues**: There were gripes about the platform lagging, especially with larger projects. Nobody likes waiting around for things to load, right?

What Makes a Great Lucidchart Alternative?
------------------------------------------

If you're thinking about jumping ship, here's what to keep an eye out for:

*   **Affordability**: Look for tools that offer a fair price for the features you need.
*   **Ease of Use**: The last thing you want is to wrestle with a complicated interface. Go for something that feels natural.
*   **Performance**: Make sure the tool can [handle big projects](https://www.flowlu.com/features/projects/)
     without slowing down.
*   **Collaboration Features**: If you're working with a team, [real-time collaboration](https://www.flowlu.com/features/collaboration/)
     is a must.

How to Choose the Right Alternative
-----------------------------------

Picking the perfect tool isn't a one-size-fits-all deal. Here's how I'd go about it:

1.  **Identify Your Needs:** What are you using the tool for? Simple mind maps or complex diagrams? Knowing this will narrow down your options.
2.  **Set a Budget**: How much are you willing to spend? There are free tools out there, but sometimes paying a bit gets you those extra features you need.
3.  **Test Drive**: Most platforms offer free trials. Give them a whirl to see which one feels right.
4.  **Check Reviews**: See what other users are saying. Their experiences can shed light on things you might not have considered.

Remember, the best tool is the one that fits your specific needs and feels right to you. Happy diagramming!

The Best Lucidchart Competitors & Alternatives for Diagramming in 2025
----------------------------------------------------------------------

![Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/31510/flowlu_4.png)

Flowlu

Your all-in-one work management solution

[Try For Free](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

Flowlu brings together team collaboration, client and project management, document generation, knowledge sharing, mind maps, and financial tracking, all in one place. With Flowlu, you can automate those repetitive tasks that slow you down. Rely on real-time reporting to get insights into what’s working and where you can improve. Flowlu offers multiple task views—like Kanban, list, or calendar—so you can visualize work the way that suits your team best. With all your data in one place, making informed decisions is simpler than ever. It's all about reducing the effort of daily tasks, so you can focus on what really matters—growing your business and delivering value to your clients.

![ClickUp](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32100/clickup.png)

ClickUp

Customizable productivity hub

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/clickup/)

ClickUp is an all-in-one productivity platform where you can manage tasks, docs, goals, and more. It’s designed to replace multiple apps by combining project management, communication, and automation in one place. Customizable views and extensive integrations help teams tailor their workspace to fit their requirements.

![Notion](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36012/notion.png)

Notion

Organized Team Documentation

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/notion/)

Notion combines note-taking, project management, and document collaboration in one place. You can create company wikis, store contracts, and build custom workflows using databases and templates. With real-time collaboration, task tracking, and integration with tools like Slack and Google Calendar, it’s great for keeping everything organized.

![Confluence](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36133/confluence.png)

Confluence

Deep Integration With Jira

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/confluence/)

A go-to tool for teams that live in documentation. It’s where meeting notes, project plans, and company policies all end up. The interface can feel a bit cluttered, but it gets the job done—especially if you’re already using Jira or other Atlassian tools. Has powerful permissions settings, templates for different types of content, and strong version history tracking.

![Miro](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36216/miro.jpg)

Miro

Infinite Collaborative Whiteboard

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/miro/)

Miro is like a giant digital whiteboard that never runs out of space. You can sketch out ideas, add sticky notes, and work with your team in real time—no mess, no dry-erase markers dying mid-brainstorm. It’s great for planning, organizing, and making sense of scattered thoughts without getting lost in a sea of tabs. The best part? It’s packed with templates and integrations, so you can go from blank board to structured plan fast.

![Mural](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36218/mural_2x.png)

Mural

Visual Brainstorming for Teams

Mural is built for teamwork, making brainstorming feel less like a chaotic mess and more like a structured conversation. You can drop in sticky notes, drawings, and diagrams, all while collaborating in real time. It’s great for remote teams who need a space to share ideas without talking over each other. The built-in facilitation tools, like timers and voting, help keep discussions on track and productive.

![FigJam](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36219/figjam_2x.png)

FigJam

Quick Sketching & Ideas

FigJam is Figma’s more casual, brainstorming-friendly sidekick. It’s a space to scribble, sketch, and throw around ideas without worrying about perfection. Think of it as a digital napkin for quick thoughts, with just enough structure to keep things organized. The interactive widgets—like stamps and emotes—make it fun without getting in the way of real work.

![Whimsical](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36221/whimsical_2x.png)

Whimsical

Fast & Clean Mind Mapping

Whimsical combines mind mapping, flowcharts, wireframes, and documents into one clean, no-fuss workspace. It’s perfect for turning messy ideas into structured plans without getting lost in complicated menus. The lightweight design keeps things fast, so you can focus on thinking instead of wrestling with features. The built-in collaboration tools make it easy to work with others without slowing things down.

![MindMeister](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36222/mindmeister_2x.png)

MindMeister

Organized Visual Thinking

MindMeister is all about visualizing ideas in a way that actually makes sense. You can map out thoughts, add images, and even turn ideas into tasks without switching between apps. It’s great for planning projects or just getting thoughts out of your head and onto a screen. The real-time collaboration means brainstorming with a team feels natural, even when working remotely.

![XMind](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36223/xmind.jpg)

XMind

Structured Mind Mapping Tool

XMind is a solid choice for mind mapping, offering a mix of structure and creativity. Whether you’re planning a project, outlining an article, or just trying to sort through ideas, it keeps everything connected and easy to follow. It’s packed with templates, so you don’t have to start from scratch every time. The Zen mode helps keep distractions away when you need to focus.

![Coggle](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36224/coggle_2x.png)

Coggle

Simple & Colorful Maps

Coggle makes mind mapping simple, colorful, and surprisingly fun. It’s a great way to organize thoughts, plan projects, or break down complex ideas into something easier to follow. The automatic branching keeps everything looking neat, no matter how big your map gets. Plus, since it’s cloud-based, your team can jump in and edit without any fuss.

![Draw.io](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36225/draw_io_2x.png)

Draw.io

Free & Flexible Diagramming

Draw.io is a no-frills diagramming tool that gets the job done without unnecessary complexity. It’s free, flexible, and works right in your browser—no downloads needed. Whether you’re mapping workflows or sketching out ideas, it gives you all the essential tools without locking anything behind a paywall. The integration with Google Drive and other platforms makes saving and sharing effortless.

![Creately](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36226/creately_2x.png)

Creately

Diagrams & Team Collaboration

Creately is part diagramming tool, part collaboration space. You can create flowcharts, mind maps, and wireframes while working with your team in real time. The smart connectors make it easy to build and adjust diagrams without things getting messy. The database-like structure means you can link ideas together in a way that actually makes sense.

![SmartDraw](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36227/smartdraw_2x.png)

SmartDraw

Professional Diagrams, Fast

SmartDraw makes it easy to create professional-looking diagrams without spending hours adjusting every little detail. It’s great for flowcharts, floor plans, and even organizational charts, with automation that keeps everything aligned. The built-in templates cover just about every type of diagram you might need. Plus, it works with Microsoft Office and Google Workspace, so you can drop your diagrams right into your documents.

![Stormboard](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36228/stormboard_2x.png)

Stormboard

Sticky Notes, Structured

Stormboard turns sticky note brainstorming into something a little more structured. Instead of a free-for-all whiteboard, ideas snap into place, making it easier to see connections and take action. It’s great for teams who need more than just a blank canvas but don’t want to overcomplicate things. The built-in reporting tools help turn brainstorming sessions into actual plans.

![Conceptboard](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36229/conceptboard_2x.png)

Conceptboard

Visual Collaboration Made Simple

Conceptboard is built for visual collaboration, making it easy to share and refine ideas with a team. Whether you’re sketching, adding sticky notes, or reviewing designs, it keeps everything in one place. It’s especially useful for remote teams who need a shared workspace that feels natural. The commenting and annotation tools make feedback loops smoother and more productive.

![Boardmix](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36230/boardmix_2x.png)

Boardmix

Whiteboarding & Team Planning

Boardmix is a flexible whiteboarding tool that works well for brainstorming, planning, and team collaboration. You can create mind maps, wireframes, and diagrams without jumping between different apps. The simple interface makes it easy to start, even if you’re not a design pro. The real-time editing and chat features help teams stay connected and move ideas forward quickly.

![Excalidraw](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36231/excalidraw_2x.png)

Excalidraw

Hand-Drawn Style Sketching

Excalidraw is perfect for people who want a digital whiteboard that feels hand-drawn. It’s simple, open-source, and focused on sketching out ideas without unnecessary distractions. The freehand drawing style makes diagrams look natural and less rigid. Plus, it runs entirely in the browser, so there’s nothing to install or sign up for.

![Mindomo](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36232/mindomo_2x.png)

Mindomo

Mind Mapping With Tasks

Mindomo blends mind mapping with project planning, making it great for turning ideas into action. You can add deadlines, assign tasks, and track progress—all within the same tool. It’s useful for teams who want more than just a basic brainstorming space. The ability to sync with cloud storage means you never lose track of your work.

![Gliffy](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36233/gliffy_2x.png)

Gliffy

Diagrams for Workflows

Gliffy is a simple, web-based diagramming tool that works right inside your browser. It’s great for flowcharts, wireframes, and network diagrams, with an easy-to-use drag-and-drop interface. Since it integrates with Confluence and Jira, it’s a solid choice for teams already using those tools. The revision history helps track changes, so you can always roll back if needed.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

Is Flowlu a good replacement for Lucidchart?

Flowlu is a solid choice if you're looking for an all-in-one business management tool that includes mind mapping and diagramming. While it doesn’t focus solely on flowcharts like Lucidchart, it offers mind maps, project management, and collaboration tools in one platform. If you need more than just diagrams—like task tracking and team workflows—Flowlu could be a great alternative. But if you’re after a dedicated diagramming tool, you might want to check out other options.

What is the best free Lucidchart alternative?

If you need a free alternative, Draw.io (diagrams.net) is a top pick. It’s open-source, has a simple interface, and integrates well with Google Drive and OneDrive. For more advanced features, Miro and Whimsical offer free plans with limited functionality, but they work well for brainstorming and simple diagrams.

Why do people switch from Lucidchart to other tools?

Many users look for alternatives because of pricing, performance issues, and limited free features. Some find it slow when working on large projects, while others prefer a more affordable or intuitive tool. If you need better collaboration features, a lower price, or a faster interface, it might be worth exploring other options.

# Top 20 Insightly Alternatives ✅ You Must Consider

Top 20 Alternatives to Insightly for Enhanced CRM Needs
=======================================================

![Flowlu Team](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24365/zaglushka.png)

Flowlu Team

December 14, 2024

34 min read

Table of contents

*   [Reasons to Seek Insightly CRM Alternatives](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#title_0)
    
*   [The 20 Best Insightly Competitors and Alternatives in 2025](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#title_1)
    
*   [How to Choose the Right CRM Alternative to Insightly](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#title_2)
    
*   [Conclusion](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#title_3)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Top 20 Alternatives to Insightly for Enhanced CRM Needs](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/insightly.776x0.webp)

Businesses of all sizes seek the perfect customer relationship management system to streamline their operations, enhance customer interactions, and drive sales. While Insightly has been a popular choice for many, a growing number of companies are exploring alternatives to better meet their unique needs.

Insightly is known for its robust feature set, including lead management, sales tracking, and detailed reporting. It integrates well with a suite of other applications, making it a versatile tool for businesses of various sizes. However, there are times when organizations might look for Insightly alternatives that better align with their specific requirements.

Reasons to Seek Insightly CRM Alternatives
------------------------------------------

Despite its strengths, several factors might lead a business to seek Insightly analogues:

**Pricing Concerns:** For some businesses, especially small startups or those with tight budgets, Insightly's pricing structure might be a concern. As businesses grow and their needs become more complex, the costs associated with additional features and higher-tier plans can become significant.

**Feature Limitations**: Although Insightly offers a range of features, some businesses might find it lacking in certain advanced functionalities. For instance, bigger enterprises or businesses with complex sales cycles might require more sophisticated analytics, automation, or customization options than what Insightly provides.

**Integration Challenges:** Insightly integrates with a variety of applications, but some businesses might require deeper or more specific integrations with their existing software ecosystem. If Insightly doesn't natively support these integrations, businesses might look for a customer relationship management platform that does.

**Unique Business Needs:** Every business has unique needs based on its industry, customer base, sales processes, and internal workflows. A company might seek an alternative to Insightly if it offers specific features tailored to their niche requirements, such as real estate, healthcare, or manufacturing-focused CRMs.

**Data Management and Reporting:** Effective data management and reporting capabilities are vital for businesses to track performance and make informed decisions. If companies find Insightly's reporting tools insufficiently robust or flexible for their data analysis needs, they might look for a customer relationship management tool with more advanced reporting features.

The 20 Best Insightly Competitors and Alternatives in 2025
----------------------------------------------------------

When considering replacements to Insightly, businesses should carefully evaluate their specific needs, budget constraints, and long-term goals to ensure they choose a customer relationship management solution that aligns with their strategic objectives and operational requirements. Here are some top alternatives to Insightly, each with its unique strengths and offerings.

### #1: Flowlu

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/flowlu-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

[Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/)
 is known for its wide array of features that cater to various aspects of business operations. It combines [project management](https://www.flowlu.com/features/projects/)
, [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/features/crm/)
, invoicing, and [knowledge management](https://www.flowlu.com/features/knowledge-base/)
 within a single platform, providing teams with the tools they need to collaborate effectively, manage customer relationships, and keep track of financial performance. Its intuitive interface and cloud-based setup make it accessible for teams of all sizes and types, from freelancers to large enterprises.

#### Pros:

*   **Comprehensive Toolset:** Flowlu offers an extensive range of features, including project management, CRM, client portal, invoicing, and expense tracking, making it a versatile platform for managing different business functions.
*   **Collaboration-Focused:** With features like team chat, task commenting, and real-time notifications, Flowlu promotes effective team collaboration and communication.
*   **Customization and Scalability:** The platform allows for significant customization in terms of workflows, fields, and dashboards, making it adaptable to the specific needs of a business as it grows.
*   **Financial Management:** Flowlu includes [financial tools](https://www.flowlu.com/features/finance/)
     such as [invoicing](https://www.flowlu.com/features/invoice/)
    , expense tracking, and financial reporting, providing businesses with a clear view of their financial health.
*   **Automation Features:** Flowlu CRM includes automation capabilities that can streamline repetitive tasks and processes, enhancing team efficiency and productivity.

#### Cons:

*   **Learning Curve:** Given its broad range of functionalities, new users may require some time to fully grasp all aspects of the platform and make the most of its extensive feature set.
*   **Potentially Overwhelming for Small Teams:** Smaller teams or businesses with simpler needs might find the array of features more than they need, leading to underutilization of the platform. In order to overcome such a problem, it is recommended to consider implementation services to unlock Flowlu’s full potential.

#### 

#### Pricing:

Flowlu's pricing structure is designed to accommodate a range of business sizes and needs, starting with a free plan that includes basic customer relationship management functionality and project management features for up to 2 users.

Paid plans begin with the Team plan at $29/month (billed annually), which expands user access and includes more advanced features. The Business plan at $59/month offers even more functionality, including automation workflows and financial management tools. For larger organizations, the Professional plan at $119/month provides access to the full suite of Flowlu's features, catering to the needs of large teams and complex projects.

In summary, Flowlu is a robust alternative to Insightly, especially for businesses seeking a platform that combines CRM with project and financial management. Its comprehensive feature set, focus on collaboration, and scalability make it a compelling choice for a variety of business types and sizes.

### #2: HubSpot CRM

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/hubspot-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [HubSpot CRM](https://www.hubspot.com/products/crm)
_

HubSpot CRM is celebrated for its ease of use and seamless integration with the broader HubSpot ecosystem, encompassing marketing, sales, and service hubs. This customer relationship management system is particularly attractive for small to medium-sized businesses looking for a cost-effective solution that offers basic CRM functionalities for free, with the option to upgrade as the business grows. Its intuitive interface and automated data capture reduce manual entry, saving time and minimizing errors.

#### Pros:

*   User-friendly interface, great for CRM beginners.
*   Free tier available with essential CRM features.
*   Strong marketing and sales integration within the HubSpot ecosystem.

#### Cons:

*   Costs can escalate quickly as you add paid features or modules.
*   Customization options are more limited compared to some competitors.
*   The free plan includes HubSpot branding.

#### Pricing:

HubSpot CRM starts with a free plan, with paid plans beginning at $45/month (billed annually) for the Starter package, which includes additional sales and marketing features.

### #3: Zoho CRM

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/zoho-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Zoho CRM](https://www.zoho.com/crm/)
_

Zoho CRM is another powerful contender, known for its affordability and flexibility. It caters to businesses of all sizes and offers a wide range of features, including sales automation, lead management, analytics, and a customizable platform. Zoho CRM stands out for its AI-powered assistant, Zia, which provides sales predictions, anomaly detection, and suggestions for the best times to contact leads, enhancing the efficiency of sales teams.

#### Pros:

*   Good balance of features and affordability.
*   Includes AI-powered sales assistant (Zia) for insights and suggestions.
*   Flexible customization and automation options.

#### Cons:

*   The interface can feel cluttered with advanced features.
*   Learning curve for utilizing the full suite of tools.
*   Integration with non-Zoho apps can be more complex.

#### Pricing:

Zoho CRM offers a free edition for up to 3 users, with paid plans starting from $14/user/month (billed annually) for the Standard plan.

### #4: Pipedrive

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/pipedrive-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Pipedrive](https://www.pipedrive.com/)
_

[Pipedrive](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/pipedrive/)
 shines as a user-friendly customer relationship management platform designed by salespeople, for salespeople. Its visual sales pipeline gives clear visibility into the status of various deals, and its easy-to-use interface ensures that sales teams can focus on the right leads at the right time. Pipedrive is particularly suited for small to medium-sized businesses looking for a straightforward tool that emphasizes sales management and tracking.

#### Pros:

*   Intuitive and visual sales pipeline management.
*   Customizable pipelines and fields to fit various sales processes.
*   Strong mobile app for sales tracking on the go.

#### Cons:

*   Limited functionality beyond sales and pipeline management.
*   Reporting features could be more advanced.
*   Lacks built-in email marketing tools.

#### Pricing:

Pipedrive's pricing starts at $12.50/user/month (billed annually) for the Essential plan, with more advanced features available in higher-tier plans.

### #5: Salesforce

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/salesforce.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Salesforce](https://www.salesforce.com/)
_

[Salesforce](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/salesforce/)
 stands as a giant in the CRM world, known for its extensive customization options, powerful analytics, and a wide array of integrations with third-party applications. Its cloud-based platform is designed to serve businesses of all sizes, making it a versatile choice for those looking to scale. Salesforce's robust set of features includes sales management, marketing automation, partner relationship management, and customer service, making it a comprehensive suite for managing all facets of customer relationships.

#### Pros:

*   Highly customizable to suit a wide range of business needs.
*   Extensive ecosystem with numerous integrations and add-ons.
*   Powerful analytics and reporting capabilities.

#### Cons:

*   Can be complex and overwhelming for small businesses.
*   Higher cost, especially when adding multiple integrations and customizations.
*   Requires training to fully leverage its vast functionalities.

#### Pricing:

Salesforce offers several pricing tiers starting from the Essentials plan at $25/user/month (billed annually) to more advanced plans that cater to larger enterprises.

### #6: Freshsales

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/freshsales-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Freshsales](https://www.freshworks.com/crm/sales/)
_

[Freshsales](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/freshsales/)
, by Freshworks, is a dynamic customer relationship management solution known for its straightforward UI and AI-driven insights. It offers a 360-degree view of the customer, capturing customer interactions across email, phone, and chat in a single thread. With features like lead scoring, visual deal pipelines, and automated workflows, Freshsales aims to help sales teams close deals faster and foster meaningful customer relationships.

#### Pros:

*   AI-powered insights for lead scoring and deal predictions.
*   Offers a free tier with basic CRM functionalities.

#### Cons:

### #7: Agile CRM

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/agilecrm-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Agile CRM](https://www.agilecrm.com/)
_

Agile CRM is an all-in-one customer relationship management tool that offers sales, marketing, and service automation in a single platform. It's designed for small and medium-sized businesses looking to streamline their operations without the complexity of larger CRMs. With features like contact management, telephony, and gamification, Agile CRM focuses on enhancing productivity and customer engagement.

### #8: Copper

_

Formerly known as ProsperWorks, Copper is renowned for its deep integration with Google Workspace, making it an ideal choice for organizations heavily utilizing Google applications. Copper automates data entry by capturing information from emails and calendar events, facilitating a more efficient workflow for sales teams.

*   User-friendly interface that closely mirrors Google's design, making it easy for teams to adopt.
*   Automates data entry and offers workflow automation to save time.

#### Cons:

*   Limited customization options compared to more comprehensive customer relationship management solutions.

### #9: Apptivo

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/apptivo-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Apptivo](https://www.apptivo.com/)
_

Apptivo stands out for its suite of over 50 integrated business applications, encompassing customer relationship management, project management, invoicing, and supply chain management. This versatility makes Apptivo a comprehensive solution for businesses looking to manage various aspects of their operations within a single platform. Its customization capabilities allow businesses to tailor the software to their specific processes and workflows.

#### Pros:

*   Offers a wide array of integrated business apps beyond CRM, providing an all-in-one business management solution.
*   Highly customizable, allowing businesses to tailor the platform to their specific needs.
*   Competitive pricing makes it accessible for small to medium-sized businesses.

#### Cons:

*   With the vast array of features and apps, the learning curve can be steep for new users.
*   Some users may find the interface less intuitive compared to other customer relationship management solutions.
*   Integration with non-Apptivo apps and services could be more streamlined.

#### Pricing:

Apptivo's pricing starts with a free Starter plan, with more comprehensive features available in the Premium plan at $10/user/month and the Ultimate plan at $25/user/month, billed annually.

### #10: Streak

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/streak-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Streak](https://www.streak.com/)
_

Streak is a unique CRM that operates entirely within Gmail, turning your inbox into a powerful customer relationship management system. It's particularly suited for small businesses and solopreneurs who prefer to manage their sales and customer relationships directly from their email. Streak offers pipeline views, email tracking, and collaboration tools, making it easy to manage deals without leaving your inbox.

#### Pros:

*   Fully integrated within Gmail, making it an excellent choice for users who prefer to manage their workflows directly from their email.
*   Offers a unique approach to CRM with its pipeline-focused email organization.
*   The free version includes basic customer relationship management features suitable for individuals and small teams.

#### Cons:

*   Being confined to Gmail might be limiting for businesses looking for a more traditional, standalone CRM system.
*   The customization and reporting capabilities are somewhat limited compared to full-fledged CRM platforms.
*   May not scale as well for larger businesses with complex CRM needs.

#### Pricing:

Streak offers a free plan with basic customer relationship management features, with paid plans starting at $15/user/month for the Professional plan, which includes more advanced CRM functionalities.

### #11: Bitrix24

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/bitrix24-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Bitrix24](https://www.bitrix24.com/)
_

Bitrix24 offers a wide array of tools, including CRM, task and project management, communication, and website building capabilities. It's designed for businesses of all sizes looking for a comprehensive platform to manage various aspects of their operations. Bitrix24's strong collaboration features, such as group chat, video conferencing, and document management, make it a solid choice for teams looking for integrated communication and management solutions.

#### Pros:

*   Provides a comprehensive suite of tools including CRM, task management, project management, communication, and website building.
*   Offers a generous free plan for up to 12 users, making it an attractive option for small businesses.
*   Features strong collaboration tools, such as group chat and video conferencing.

#### Cons:

*   The interface can be overwhelming due to the multitude of features, resulting in a steeper learning curve.
*   Some advanced features and integrations are only available in higher-tier plans.
*   Customization options may be extensive but can require time to set up properly.

#### Pricing:

Bitrix24's free plan supports unlimited users, with paid plans starting at $19/month for the Start+ plan, going up to $159/month for the CRM+ plan, catering to more extensive business needs.

### #12: Nimble

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/nimble-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Nimble](https://www.nimble.com/)
_

Nimble is a social CRM that excels in integrating social media insights with traditional customer relationship management functionalities. It automatically updates customer profiles with social media interactions, providing a more holistic view of customer engagement. Nimble's browser extension allows for easy contact management and sales pipeline activities directly from your email inbox or any social media platform.

#### Pros:

*   Strong social media integration, allowing businesses to enrich contact profiles with social insights.
*   Easy to use with a clean interface and straightforward functionality.
*   Offers a browser extension that works across the web for easy contact management.

#### Cons:

*   The focus on social CRM features may not meet the needs of businesses looking for more traditional CRM functionalities.
*   Limited customization options for sales processes and pipelines.
*   Reporting and analytics features might not be as comprehensive as those found in more specialized CRM solutions.

#### Pricing:

Nimble offers a straightforward pricing model at $19/user/month when billed annually, with access to all core features.

### #13: Capsule CRM

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/capsulecrm-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Capsule](https://capsulecrm.com/)
_

Capsule focuses on simplicity and ease of use, offering an intuitive platform for small to medium-sized businesses to manage their contacts, sales pipelines, and tasks. Its customization options allow for a tailored customer relationship management experience, and its integration with popular applications like G Suite, Mailchimp, and Xero extends its functionality.

#### Pros:

*   Simple and intuitive design, making it easy for teams to onboard and use the system effectively.
*   Good integration capabilities with popular apps and services, enhancing its functionality.
*   Focuses on essential CRM features, making it a good fit for small businesses that need straightforward customer management.

#### Cons:

*   Limited advanced features and customization, which might not satisfy the needs of larger organizations or those with complex workflows.
*   The reporting and analytics capabilities are somewhat basic compared to more advanced systems.
*   May not offer as extensive automation features as some competitors.

#### Pricing:

Capsule offers a free plan for up to 2 users, with the Professional plan priced at $18/user/month, providing more advanced customer relationship management features.

### #14: Close

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/close-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Close](https://www.close.com/)
_

Close is designed with the philosophy of keeping sales teams selling, offering a CRM that's packed with productivity tools like email automation, calling, and SMS. Its streamlined interface and powerful search functionality make it easy for sales teams to focus on their interactions with prospects and customers, rather than managing the CRM.

#### Pros:

*   Emphasizes sales communication with built-in calling, SMS, and email capabilities, streamlining outreach efforts.
*   Offers a highly intuitive interface focused on sales productivity and efficiency.
*   Strong automation features to reduce repetitive tasks and focus on selling.

#### Cons:

*   Primarily focused on sales, which might limit its use as a comprehensive CRM solution for businesses looking for broader functionalities.
*   Pricing can be on the higher side for small businesses, especially when considering the most advanced features.
*   Limited third-party integrations compared to some other customer relationship management platforms.

#### Pricing:

Close's pricing starts at $25/user/month for the Starter plan, with more comprehensive plans available for businesses with advanced sales needs.

### #15: Scoro

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/scoro-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Scoro](https://www.scoro.com/)
_

Scoro provides a comprehensive work management solution that includes CRM, project management, quoting, billing, and advanced reporting features. It's particularly suited for professional service businesses and agencies looking for a platform to manage the entire workflow from lead to billing.

#### Pros:

*   Offers a broad set of features beyond CRM, including project management, billing, and time tracking, providing a holistic business management tool.
*   Highly customizable dashboards and reports allow businesses to tailor the platform to their needs.
*   Strong focus on productivity and efficiency with tools designed to streamline business processes.

#### Cons:

*   The wide array of features and options can make the platform overwhelming for new users.
*   Higher price point, especially for smaller businesses or those just looking for a basic CRM.
*   Some users may find the mobile app less comprehensive compared to the desktop version.

Scoro's pricing starts at $26/user/month for the Essential plan, with more advanced features and capabilities available in higher-tier plans.

### #16: Microsoft Dynamics 365

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24349/microsoft-sales-min.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/insightly/#popup)

_via [Dynamics 365](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/dynamics-365)
_

For those deeply entrenched in the Microsoft ecosystem, Dynamics 365 offers a customer relationship management solution that integrates seamlessly with other Microsoft products, such as Office 365, Outlook, and Teams. Its comprehensive suite includes sales, customer service, field service, and project service automation, making it a robust choice for businesses looking for an enterprise-level customer relationship management solution with strong customization and integration capabilities.

#### Pros:

*   Deep integration with Microsoft products like Office 365 and Teams.
*   Highly customizable and scalable for large enterprises.
*   Offers a wide range of additional business apps within the ecosystem.

#### Cons:
