Teamwork is a project management platform designed to streamline team collaboration. It allows companies to manage tasks, track time, and stay on top of project timelines with ease. With its client-focused features, Teamwork makes managing complex projects and keeping everyone aligned simple.

![Harvest](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36038/harvest_2x.png)

Harvest

Track Time, Budget, And Invoices

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/harvest/)

Harvest keeps things simple: track time, send invoices, and keep an eye on budgets without extra hassle. It works well for teams that need to track billable hours and project costs. Plus, the reporting makes it easy to see where time (and money) is going.

![Clockify](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36039/clockify.png)

Clockify

Free Time Tracking For Teams

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/clockify/)

Clockify is a solid pick if you want a free time tracker that works for individuals and teams. It lets you log hours, categorize tasks, and pull reports to see where time is being spent. You can also set hourly rates and track billable hours, making it useful for freelancers and businesses alike.

![RescueTime](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36041/rescuetime_2x.png)

RescueTime

Automatic Tracking For Better Focus

RescueTime runs in the background and tracks how you actually spend your time on your computer and phone. Instead of manual entries, it automatically logs time spent on different apps and websites. It’s great for figuring out where your focus goes and cutting down on distractions.

![Everhour](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36042/everhour_2x.png)

Everhour

Track Time Inside Project Tools

Everhour works well if you need time tracking built into your project management tool. It integrates with apps like Asana and Trello, so you can track time without switching tabs. Plus, it helps with budgeting and invoicing, making it useful for teams working on billable projects.

![TimeCamp](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36043/timecamp_2x.png)

TimeCamp

Smart Tracking With Productivity Insights

TimeCamp is built for tracking both billable and non-billable hours, with automatic tracking to fill in the gaps. It also has productivity reports and integrations with popular project management tools. If you need a mix of time tracking and budget monitoring, it covers both.

![Hubstaff](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36044/hubstaff_2x.png)

Hubstaff

Time Tracking With Team Monitoring

Hubstaff is a time tracker with built-in productivity monitoring, making it useful for remote teams. It logs time, tracks activity levels, and even has GPS tracking for field teams. It’s a good fit if you need to keep an eye on work hours and productivity in one place.

![FreshBooks](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36045/freshbooks_2x.png)

FreshBooks

Track Time And Invoice Clients

FreshBooks is known for invoicing, but it also has built-in time tracking. You can log hours, attach them to projects, and turn them into invoices with a few clicks. It’s handy for freelancers and small businesses that need to track billable hours and get paid without extra steps.

![QuickBooks Time](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36046/quickbooks_time_2x.png)

QuickBooks Time

Employee Tracking With Payroll Sync

QuickBooks Time (formerly TSheets) is a time tracker that works well for businesses managing hourly employees. It has GPS tracking for teams on the go and integrates with QuickBooks for easy payroll processing. Whether you’re tracking work hours or billable time, it keeps things organized.

![Clockodo](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/36047/clockodo_2x.png)

Clockodo

Easy Tracking With Project Reports

Clockodo is a straightforward time tracker that helps small businesses and freelancers keep track of hours worked. You can track time by client, project, or task, and the reporting makes it easy to see where time is going. It also integrates with invoicing tools to simplify billing.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

What is the best Toggl alternative for freelancers?

If you’re a freelancer looking for a simple, no-fuss time tracker, Clockify and RescueTime are solid picks. Clockify offers unlimited tracking for free, while RescueTime helps you understand where your time really goes. If invoicing is a must, Harvest is another great option.

Is Flowlu a good replacement for Toggl?

Yes, Flowlu is a strong Toggl alternative—especially if you need more than just time tracking. Unlike Toggl, Flowlu offers built-in project management, invoicing, and CRM features, making it a great all-in-one tool for businesses and teams. If you’re looking for a tool that tracks time _and_ keeps your projects organized, Flowlu is definitely worth considering.

What’s the easiest time tracking tool to use?

If you want something super intuitive, Clockify and Timely are great options. Clockify keeps it simple with a free, unlimited tracker, while Timely automates tracking, so you don’t have to remember to start and stop a timer. Both are great for those who don’t want to overcomplicate time tracking.

# Best TeamGantt Alternatives | Top Project Management Tools

The Best TeamGantt Alternatives to Boost Your Projects
======================================================

![Ethan Kim](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/33122/ethan_kim-1.png)

Ethan Kim

Content Specialist

December 12, 2024

8 min read

Table of contents

*   [Why Consider Options Beyond TeamGantt?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamgantt/#title_0)
    
*   [What to Look for in a TeamGantt Alternative](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamgantt/#title_1)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![The Best TeamGantt Alternatives to Boost Your Projects](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/33104/teamgantt.776x0.webp)

Effective project management often depends on tools that are both easy to navigate and equipped with vital features. TeamGantt has gained traction for its straightforward Gantt chart style, providing a clear snapshot of project timelines.

However, as teams expand or their requirements evolve, exploring other tools that offer added flexibility, enhanced features, or a new approach may be beneficial. Let’s look at why you might consider alternatives and key aspects to consider in your search.

Why Consider Options Beyond TeamGantt?
--------------------------------------

Though TeamGantt is a powerful platform, some specific limitations may lead teams to explore other tools:

*   **Integration Range:** TeamGantt supports integrations, like with Trello, but offers fewer connections than many competing tools. This can make it difficult to integrate smoothly with all the other apps a team may need.
*   **Customization Flexibility:** The simplicity of TeamGantt is appealing, but teams needing tailored workflows or custom fields might find it limiting. For specialized projects, the setup might feel restrictive.
*   **Mobile Functionality:** While a mobile app is available, users sometimes find it less intuitive than the desktop version, which can make complex task management on mobile challenging.
*   **Pricing for Larger Teams:** TeamGantt’s pricing generally works for smaller groups, but costs can rise as the team expands, which may lead teams to seek more budget-friendly options.
*   **Collaboration Tools:** Basic task assignments and discussions are available, but TeamGantt lacks some real-time collaboration capabilities, which can mean using additional tools.

What to Look for in a TeamGantt Alternative
-------------------------------------------

If you’re searching for the right replacement, consider the features that will best support your team’s workflow. Key points include:

*   **Smooth Integrations:** Choose tools that connect well with essential software like Slack or Google Drive, helping keep everything in sync.
*   **Flexible Workflow Options:** Look for features that let you customize fields, set dependencies, and adjust views to match your company's specific processes and workflows.
*   **User-Friendly Interface:** An intuitive design minimizes the learning curve, allowing your teammates to dive into project management quickly and efficiently.
*   **Budget-Friendliness:** Make sure the pricing plans align with your business's growth plans to avoid unexpected costs as you scale.
*   **Mobile Optimization:** For mobile access, a responsive app can enhance productivity, letting teams stay on top of tasks anytime.

Top Alternatives to TeamGantt for Streamlined Project Management
----------------------------------------------------------------

Flowlu offers multiple task views—like Kanban, list, or calendar—so you can visualize work the way that suits your team best. With all your data in one place, making informed decisions is simpler than ever. It's all about reducing the effort of daily tasks, so you can focus on what really matters—growing your business and delivering value to your clients.

# Top Todoist Alternatives for Better Task Management in 2026

The Best Todoist Alternatives for Better Productivity in 2026
=============================================================

![Oliver Carter](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35772/oliver_carter-1.png)

Oliver Carter

Copywriting Specialist

February 14, 2025

8 min read

Table of contents

*   [What is Todoist?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/todoist/#title_0)
    
*   [Where Does Todoist Fall Short?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/todoist/#title_1)
    
*   [Choosing the Best Alternative to Todoist](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/todoist/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![The Best Todoist Alternatives for Better Productivity in 2026](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35753/todoist.776x0.webp)

Todoist is a go-to for task management, but is it the best fit for you? We’re breaking down its drawbacks and what to look for in a smarter, more efficient alternative.

Managing tasks efficiently is vital for business owners and project managers. Todoist has been a popular choice for many, but it's not without its shortcomings. Let's explore what Todoist offers, where it falls short based on client feedback, and how to choose the best alternative to suit your needs.

What is Todoist?
----------------

Todoist is a task management application that helps users organize and prioritize tasks across various platforms. It's known for its user-friendly interface and cross-platform compatibility, allowing users to manage tasks on the go.

Where Does Todoist Fall Short?
------------------------------

Despite its strengths, Todoist customers have pointed out several areas where it could improve:

*   **Limited Free Version**: Some users feel that essential features are restricted to the paid version. This can be a drawback for those who want comprehensive free tools.
*   **Subtasks**: Handling subtasks can be cumbersome. This makes it difficult to manage complex projects effectively.
*   **Integrations**: There are reports of limited integrations with other tools. Certainly, this can hinder seamless management of your process.
*   **Customization**: Users have expressed a desire for more customizable widgets and the ability to consolidate tasks, project management, and calendars into a single widget.
*   **Collaboration**: Some Todoist clients find the collaboration features lacking. They said that it is difficult to integrate the software within teams or groups.

Choosing the Best Alternative to Todoist
----------------------------------------

When considering a switch, keep these factors in mind:

1.  **Feature Set**: You need to make sure that the tool offers [comprehensive task management](https://www.flowlu.com/features/tasks/)
     options. It should include subtasks, reminders, task automation, and recurring tasks.
2.  **User Experience**: If your new tool has a clean, intuitive interface, this can make a significant difference in daily use.
3.  **Integration Capabilities**: You should look for software that integrates well with other tools you use, such as calendars, communication platforms, and file storage services.
4.  **Collaboration Tools**: If you're working with a team, features like shared projects, real-time updates, and [communication channels](https://www.flowlu.com/features/collaboration/)
     are essential.
5.  **Pricing**: Consider whether the tool offers a free version or trial, and evaluate the value provided in the paid tiers.

By focusing on these criteria, you can find a task management tool that better aligns with your specific needs and enhances your productivity.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

What are the top free alternatives to Todoist?

Many users seek free task management tools that offer advanced features comparable to Todoist. Some popular tools similar to Todoist include Flowlu, Trello, and Asana. These platforms provide various functionalities such as task tracking, collaboration tools, and integrations with other applications.

Which task management apps offer better collaboration features than Todoist?

For enhanced team collaboration, tools like Flowlu and ClickUp are often recommended. They offer features such as real-time updates, shared workspaces, and advanced communication tools that facilitate better teamwork.

Are there task management tools with more customization options than Todoist?

Yes, platforms like Flowlu and Notion provide extensive customization options. Users can tailor their workflows, create custom fields, and design personalized dashboards to suit their specific needs.

# Teamwork Alternatives: Top 10 ✅ Project Management Solutions

10 Best Teamwork Alternatives for Project Management
====================================================

![Flowlu Team](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24316/zaglushka.png)

Flowlu Team

January 25, 2025

22 min read

Table of contents

*   [6 Reasons Why Businesses Look for Alternatives to Teamwork](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#title_0)
    
*   [Top 10 Teamwork Competitors in 2025](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#title_1)
    
*   [Transitioning Tips](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#title_2)
    
*   [Conclusion](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#title_3)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![10 Best Teamwork Alternatives for Project Management](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/teamwork.776x0.webp)

Navigating the maze of project management tools can feel like searching for a needle in a haystack, especially when you're looking for the perfect fit for your team's diverse needs. Teamwork has been a beacon for many organizations, guiding them towards more structured and efficient project handling.

However, the landscape of project management is as varied as it is vast, and what works for one team may not be the ideal solution for another. Let’s explore top 10 alternatives to Teamwork and shed light on the plethora of options available, helping you pinpoint the tool that resonates most with your team's workflow and objectives.

Teamwork is a project management and team collaboration software that has been designed to help teams organize their work, track progress, and achieve better outcomes. It provides a suite of tools that facilitate task assignment, milestone setting, time tracking, and file sharing, among other functionalities. Teamwork is known for its user-friendly interface and flexibility, making it a popular choice among businesses of various sizes and industries.

6 Reasons Why Businesses Look for Alternatives to Teamwork
----------------------------------------------------------

**#1: Cost Considerations:** While Teamwork offers a range of pricing plans, including a free tier, the cost can escalate as teams grow and require access to more advanced features. Smaller businesses or startups might find the Teamwork’ pricing prohibitive over time.

**#2: Feature Specificity:** Some teams might require specialized functionalities that are not available in Teamwork or are not as developed as they would prefer. This can include more advanced reporting tools, specific integration needs, client portal, knowledge base, customer relationship management or unique workflow customization options.

**#3: User Experience:** Although Teamwork is designed to be user-friendly, the user experience can be subjective, and some teams might find the interface less intuitive or the navigation more complex compared to other solutions. This can lead to a steeper learning curve for some Teamwork users.

**#4: Performance and Scalability:** As projects become more complex and teams grow, some users might experience performance issues with the software like Teamwork. Teams requiring high scalability might look for alternatives that can more efficiently handle an increasing workload and user base.

**#5: Support and Community:** The level of customer support and the presence of an active user community can be crucial for teams relying heavily on Teamwork project management software. If users find the Teamwork’ support lacking or the community resources insufficient, they might explore other options.

**#6: Integration Capabilities**: While Teamwork provides various integrations, there may be specific tools or platforms that it does not support, leading teams that rely on these tools to seek alternatives with better integration capabilities.

In summary, Teamwork is a comprehensive project management tool that caters to a wide array of team collaboration needs. However, factors such as cost, the need for specialized features, user experience preferences, scalability concerns, support expectations, and integration requirements may drive teams to explore alternatives that better align with their specific project management challenges and objectives.

Top 10 Teamwork Competitors in 2025
-----------------------------------

A variety of Teamwork alternatives offer unique advantages. Let's delve into an overview of the leading contenders available:

### #1: Flowlu

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/flowlu-2.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#popup)

[Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/)
 provides an all-in-one business management software solution, encompassing project management, [task planning](https://www.flowlu.com/features/tasks/)
, [online invoicing](https://www.flowlu.com/features/invoice/)
,[knowledge management](https://www.flowlu.com/features/knowledge-base/)
, and [CRM](https://www.flowlu.com/features/crm/)
 functionalities, among others. It is designed to cater to the needs of small to medium-sized businesses across various industries.

Flowlu's platform is known for its comprehensive approach to managing business operations, aiming to streamline workflows, improve communication, and ensure efficient project execution.

#### Pros:

*   Powerful Task and [Project Tracking](https://www.flowlu.com/features/projects/)
    : Effective project planning and task execution are crucial for project success, and Flowlu offers a range of tools to facilitate these processes. They include time tracker, Gantt charts, Kanban boards, project workflows, advanced reports, backlog and issue tracking, and so much more.
*   Comprehensive Toolset: Flowlu offers a wide array of features beyond traditional project management, including financial management tools like invoicing and expense tracking, CRM functionalities, budgeting, document builder, and mind maps for brainstorming.
*   Customization and Flexibility: Users can customize workflows, fields, templates and task statuses to fit their specific business processes, making Flowlu adaptable to various project types and management styles.
*   Intuitive Interface: Despite its extensive features, Flowlu maintains an intuitive and user-friendly interface, facilitating easy adoption and minimizing the learning curve for new users.
*   [Collaboration Features](https://www.flowlu.com/features/collaboration/)
    : With built-in communication tools, knowledge bases, and collaboration spaces, Flowlu encourages seamless team interaction and information sharing.
*   Automation Tools: Flowlu's automation features minimize manual tasks, freeing up time for teams to focus on strategic activities. From auto-assigning tasks based on predefined rules to automating invoice generation, Flowlu ensures that your projects move forward smoothly without bottlenecks.
*   Scalability: Flowlu is designed to grow with your business, offering scalable solutions that can accommodate increasing complexity and team size.

#### Cons:

*   Learning Curve: While the interface is intuitive, the sheer breadth of functionalities can require a significant time investment to master and fully integrate into daily operations.
*   Mobile App Limitations: While Flowlu provides a user-friendly mobile app for IOS and Android with most of the popular modules, still its features may not be as extensive as in the Flowlu’s browser version.

#### 

#### Pricing

Flowlu's pricing structure is designed to cater to a wide range of businesses, from freelancers and small teams to larger organizations requiring more comprehensive features. Flowlu offers several pricing tiers to accommodate different needs and budgets:

*   Free Plan: It includes essential features, making it suitable for small teams of 2 users or individuals just starting out.
*   Team Plan: It is designed for small to medium-sized teams and includes additional features beyond the Free plan. Pricing starts at $29 per month for a team of 8 users when billed annually.
*   Business Plan: It offers an expanded set of features for bigger teams or businesses requiring more extensive project management and CRM capabilities. Pricing starts at $59 per month for a team of 16 users with annual billing.
*   Professional Plan: It is tailored for organizations needing full access to Flowlu's capabilities, including all available features and integrations. Pricing starts at $119 per month for a team of 25 users when billed annually.
*   Enterprise Plan: This plan is built for very large businesses or those with specific needs that require customized solutions. Pricing starts at $199 per month for a team with the unlimited users when billed annually.

In summary, Flowlu is a robust Teamwork alternative that offers a broad range of business and project management tools. It is particularly well-suited for businesses looking for an all-in-one solution to manage various aspects of their operations.

### #2: Monday.com

[Monday.com](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/monday-com/)
 stands out for its highly customizable workflows and intuitive interface. Ideal for teams seeking flexibility, it offers a range of pricing options to accommodate businesses of all sizes.

#### Pros:

*   Customizable Workflows: Monday.com allows users to tailor their workflows extensively, which can accommodate a wide variety of project types and team sizes.
*   Intuitive Interface: Its user-friendly design makes it easy for new users to adopt and navigate the platform.
*   Robust Integration: Offers seamless integration with numerous third-party apps, enhancing its utility and adaptability to different work environments.

#### Cons:

#### Pricing

Monday.com offers a tiered pricing structure starting with a Basic plan, followed by Standard, Pro, and Enterprise levels. Prices typically start from around $8 per user per month for the Basic plan when billed annually. The Standard plan, which is more popular, starts at approximately $10 per user per month.

### #3: Asana

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/asana.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#popup)

_via [Asana](https://asana.com/)
_

[Asana](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/asana/)
 is renowned for its straightforward task management and collaboration features. With a focus on simplicity and efficiency, it's an excellent choice for teams that prioritize ease of use.

#### Pros:

*   Ease of Use: Known for its straightforward and intuitive design, Asana makes task and project management accessible even to novices.
*   Versatile Task Management: Offers various views (list, board, timeline) to accommodate different project management styles.
*   Strong Collaboration Features: Facilitates team collaboration with features like task comments, file attachments, and integrated messaging.

#### Cons:

*   Limited Free Version: The free version is somewhat limited in features, pushing teams towards paid plans for more comprehensive functionality.
*   Complexity in Larger Projects: While great for task management, Asana can become cumbersome when managing more complex, large-scale projects.

#### Pricing

Asana provides a free Basic plan for individuals or teams just getting started. Its Premium plan starts at about $10.99 per user per month when billed annually, and the Business plan is around $24.99 per user per month with annual billing. There's also an Enterprise plan with custom pricing.

### #4: Trello

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/trello.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#popup)

_via [Trello](https://trello.com/)
_

Trello's card-centric approach excels in managing projects visually. Its simplicity and accessibility make it a favorite among teams looking for a straightforward, no-frills tool.

#### Pros:

*   User-Friendly Interface: Trello's simple, card-based layout is easy to understand and use, making it ideal for straightforward project management.
*   Flexibility: Boards, lists, and cards can be customized to fit various project needs, from content calendars to sprint planning.
*   Integration with Power-Ups: Enhance Trello's functionality with Power-Ups, allowing for integration with other tools and addition of new features.

#### Cons:

*   Limited Features in Free Version: The free version of Trello offers limited functionality, which might necessitate an upgrade to premium plans for more comprehensive features.
*   Scalability Issues: Trello can become unwieldy with an increase in project complexity and team size, making it less suitable for larger, more complex projects.

#### Pricing

Trello offers a free version with basic functionality. The Standard plan is around $5 per user per month (billed annually), the Premium plan is about $10 per user per month (billed annually), and there's an Enterprise plan that starts at $17.50 per user per month (for 250 users, with the price decreasing with more users), emphasizing larger organizations.

### #5: ClickUp

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/clickup.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#popup)

_via [ClickUp](https://clickup.com/)
_

[ClickUp](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/clickup/)
 offers a comprehensive suite of project management features, including task assignments, document sharing, and time tracking. Its versatility makes it suitable for teams with diverse needs.

#### Pros:

*   Comprehensive Feature Set: ClickUp offers an extensive range of features covering nearly every aspect of project and task management.
*   Customizability: Highly customizable, allowing teams to tailor the platform to their specific workflows and preferences.
*   All-in-One Platform: Combines tasks, docs, goals, and chat within a single platform, reducing the need for multiple tools.

#### Cons:

*   Steep Learning Curve: The wide array of features can be overwhelming, making it challenging for new users to get started.
*   Performance Issues: Users have reported that the platform can become sluggish with heavy use, potentially hindering productivity.

#### Pricing

ClickUp has a Free Forever plan with limited features. The Unlimited plan starts at $5 per member per month when billed annually. The Business plan is around $12 per member per month, and there's also a Business Plus and Enterprise plan with custom pricing.

### #6: Wrike

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/wrike.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#popup)

_via [Wrike](https://www.wrike.com/)
_

[Wrike](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/wrike/)
 is designed for teams that require a high degree of collaboration. With advanced features like real-time updates and customizable dashboards, it's ideal for complex projects.

#### Pros:

*   Advanced Collaboration Tools: Wrike excels in collaboration, offering real-time updates, document editing, and team communication within the platform.
*   Customizable Dashboards: Users can create personalized dashboards, providing a clear overview of project progress and priorities.
*   Scalability: Well-suited for larger teams and complex projects, offering features that cater to a wide range of project management needs.

#### Cons:

*   Complex Interface: The array of features and customization options can make the interface feel cluttered, leading to a steeper learning curve.
*   Cost: Advanced features and larger team capabilities come at a premium price, which might be a barrier for smaller teams or startups.

#### Pricing

Wrike offers a free plan with basic features. The Professional plan starts at $9.80 per user per month (billed annually), and the Business plan is approximately $24.80 per user per month with annual billing. Wrike also has an Enterprise plan for which the pricing is custom.

### #7: Smartsheet

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/smartsheet.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#popup)

_via [Smartsheet](https://www.smartsheet.com/)
_

Smartsheet combines the best of spreadsheet-based project management with robust collaboration tools. It's particularly well-suited for teams that are accustomed to working in spreadsheets but require more functionality.

#### Pros:

*   Spreadsheet-like Interface: Familiar spreadsheet layout makes it accessible for users comfortable with Excel, with added project management capabilities.
*   Versatile Use Cases: Suitable for a variety of project types, from simple task tracking to complex project planning and reporting.
*   Robust Automation and Integration: Features strong automation tools and integrates well with other enterprise systems.

#### Cons:

*   Limited Collaboration Features: While strong in project management, it may lack some of the real-time collaboration features found in other platforms.
*   Learning Curve: The transition from a simple spreadsheet to fully utilizing Smartsheet's advanced features can require a significant time investment.

#### Pricing

Smartsheet's pricing begins with the Pro plan at around $7 per user per month (billed annually), with a minimum of three users. The Business plan is approximately $25 per user per month, also with a minimum user requirement. Enterprise and Premier plans have custom pricing.

### #8: Jira

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/jira.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#popup)

_via [Jira](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira)
_

Jira is the go-to tool for agile software development teams. Its powerful issue tracking and project management capabilities make it essential for managing complex software projects.

#### Pros:

*   Agile and Scrum Support: Specifically designed to support Agile methodologies, making it a go-to for software development teams.
*   Powerful Issue Tracking: Robust tracking features for managing bugs, tasks, and other project elements.
*   Extensive Integration: Integrates seamlessly with a wide range of developer tools and platforms.

#### Cons:

*   Complexity: Jira's comprehensive features and customization options can be daunting for new users or non-technical teams.
*   Pricing: While it offers a free tier, scaling up with more users and features can become costly.

#### Pricing

Jira offers a free plan for up to 10 users. The Standard plan is around $7.50 per user per month, and the Premium plan is $14.50 per user per month, both with annual billing. There's also an Enterprise plan with custom pricing for larger or more complex deployments.

### #9: Basecamp

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/basecamp.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#popup)

_via [Basecamp](https://basecamp.com/)
_

Basecamp's simplicity and focus on communication make it a great option for small teams and non-technical users. Its flat pricing model is also attractive for teams looking for straightforward budgeting.

#### Pros:

*   Simplicity: Basecamp's straightforward design focuses on ease of use, making it accessible for teams of all technical levels.
*   Flat Pricing Model: Unlike many other tools that charge per user, Basecamp offers a simple, flat-rate pricing structure.
*   Strong Focus on Communication: Features like message boards and campfires facilitate clear and organized team communication.

#### Cons:

*   Limited Project Management Features: While great for communication, it may lack some of the more advanced project management features found in other platforms.
*   Not Ideal for Complex Projects: The simplicity of Basecamp might not cater well to the needs of more complex projects that require detailed task management and reporting.

#### Pricing

Basecamp has a unique pricing model with a flat rate of $99 per month for unlimited users, making it an attractive option for larger teams. They also offer a personal plan with limited features for free.

### #10: ProofHub

[![](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/24300/prooofhub.776x0.webp)](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/#popup)

_via [ProofHub](https://www.proofhub.com/)
_

ProofHub offers a balanced mix of project planning, collaboration, and reporting tools. It's an all-in-one solution for teams looking for comprehensive project management capabilities.

#### Pros:

*   All-in-One Solution: Offers a comprehensive set of tools for project planning, collaboration, and reporting within a single platform.
*   Simple Pricing Structure: ProofHub's pricing is straightforward, with plans offering unlimited users, making it cost-effective for larger teams.
*   Ease of Use: Designed to be user-friendly, ensuring teams can quickly adapt to and utilize the platform.

#### Cons:

*   Limited Customization: While it offers a range of features, there may be limited options for customizing workflows compared to more flexible platforms.
*   Feature Depth: While ProofHub provides a broad set of features, some may lack the depth and advanced capabilities provided by more specialized tools.

#### Pricing

ProofHub opts for simple, flat-rate pricing rather than per-user fees. The Essential plan is around $45 per month when billed annually, and the Ultimate Control plan is approximately $89 per month with annual billing.

It's important to note that pricing details can change, and additional features or user limits might apply to each plan. For the most accurate and current pricing information, it's best to consult a software provider’s official website or contact their sales team directly.

Transitioning Tips
------------------

When considering a switch from Teamwork to another project management tool, it's crucial to:

1.  **Assess Your Needs:** Identify the specific features and capabilities that your team requires, which might be missing in Teamwork.
2.  **Trial and Evaluation:** Take advantage of free trials offered by alternatives to get hands-on experience and assess how well they fit with your team's workflow.
3.  **Training and Support:** Ensure the chosen alternative provides adequate resources for training your team and offers reliable customer support for a smooth transition.
4.  **Data Migration:** Check the ease of data migration from Teamwork to the new tool to ensure a seamless transition without loss of important project data.
5.  **Feedback Loop**: Involve your team in the decision-making process and establish a feedback loop to address any issues or concerns during the transition.

Conclusion
----------

Selecting the right project management tool is pivotal for enhancing team productivity and project outcomes. While Teamwork is a robust platform, several alternatives may offer the specific features and capabilities your team needs.

Flowlu stands out due to its comprehensive set of features that go beyond traditional task management. It offers a unique combination of project planning, CRM, invoicing, and knowledge management, all within a single platform. The flexibility and customization options it provides make it suitable for various industries and team sizes.

Flowlu is more than just an alternative to Teamwork; it's a comprehensive solution that addresses the multifaceted needs of modern project management. Its blend of features, combined with a user-friendly interface and competitive pricing, makes it an attractive choice for teams looking to enhance their project management capabilities.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

What should I consider when choosing a Teamwork alternative?

When searching for a Teamwork alternative, consider factors like pricing, features, ease of use, integration capabilities, mobile applications and customer support.

Are there free Teamwork alternatives?

Yes, several similar to Teamwork tools offer free versions with basic features and services, such as Flowlu and Trello.

How do I ensure a smooth transition from Teamwork to a new tool?

Plan your transition carefully, provide adequate training for your team, and leverage support resources offered by the Teamwork replacement.

How easy is it to migrate from Teamwork to Flowlu?

Flowlu offers several options including a CSV import of your data and support to ensure a seamless transition from Teamwork or any other project management tool.

Why look for Teamwork analogues?

Several factors compel organizations to explore other (sometimes cheaper) options. For some companies, the cost of Teamwork may be prohibitive, while others might find it lacking in specific features essential for their operations. Scalability, ease of use, and integration capabilities also play a significant role in this decision-making process.

In the realm of collaborative technologies, finding the right tool can be extremely difficult. Among Teamwork alternatives, some programs stand out, not as mere clones, but as exceptional products tailored for both Mac and Windows users. These alternatives are designed to cater to various types of teams and work styles, with some even offering robust offline functionalities for uninterrupted productivity. 

Unlike generic sites that list countless similar tools, these selected alternatives are unique in their approach, harnessing cutting-edge technologies to enhance team collaboration and project management. Each product serves as a testament to the diversity and innovation present in today's collaborative software landscape, ensuring that every team can find a solution that fits its unique needs, except perhaps for those seeking an exact replica of Teamwork.

What should I do if the alternative tool doesn't meet my expectations?

Utilize the trial period to fully explore the tool's capabilities, and don't hesitate to reach out to customer support for assistance. If the tool still doesn't meet your needs, consider other options.

# Best Things 3 Alternatives: Top Task Managers for 2026

Best Things 3 Alternatives: Find a Task Manager That Actually Works for You
===========================================================================

![Leah Brooks](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35794/leah_brooks-1.png)

Leah Brooks

Writer

February 14, 2025

9 min read

Table of contents

*   [What is Things 3?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/things/#title_0)
    
*   [Where Does Things 3 Fall Short?](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/things/#title_1)
    
*   [Choosing the Right Alternative](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/things/#title_2)
    

Experience the power of organized workflows

Sign up for free to access Flowlu's project templates. Available for new accounts only.

[Start With Template](https://www.flowlu.com/templates/)

![Best Things 3 Alternatives: Find a Task Manager That Actually Works for You](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35776/things.776x0.webp)

Thinking of moving on from Things 3? This article explores its strengths, weaknesses, and how to find a task manager that truly fits your workflow.

Ever found yourself wondering if there's a better tool than Things 3 for managing your tasks? If so, you’re not alone. A lot of folks love Things 3, but some find it lacking in a few key areas. Especially when it comes to flexibility and collaboration.

Task management is personal. Some people need a simple to-do list, while others want a full-blown productivity system that syncs across devices, integrates with their favorite apps, and plays nicely with teams. Things 3 is great for many, but it’s not for everyone. And if you’ve ever felt a little boxed in by its limitations, you’re probably thinking about switching.

Let’s break it all down: what makes Things 3 great, where it falls short, and how to find an alternative that actually fits how you work.

What is Things 3?
-----------------

Things 3 is a task management app designed exclusively for Apple devices. It's known for its clean design and intuitive interface, making it a favorite among many for organizing both personal and professional tasks. Users appreciate its simplicity and the one-time purchase model, which means no recurring subscription fees.

Where Does Things 3 Fall Short?
-------------------------------

Despite its strengths, Things 3 has some limitations that might make you consider other options:

*   **Platform Limitation:** It's only available on Apple devices. If you or your team use Windows or Android, you're out of luck.
*   **Lack of Collaboration Features:** Things 3 is primarily designed for individual use. It doesn't offer robust collaboration tools, which can be a dealbreaker for team projects.
*   **Limited Integrations:** While it offers some integrations, it's not as versatile as other apps in connecting with a wide range of tools and services.

These limitations have been highlighted in user reviews on platforms like Capterra and G2.

Choosing the Right Alternative
------------------------------

So, you're thinking about making a switch. Here's how to find the best alternative:

1.  **Identify Your Needs:** Do you need cross-platform access? Advanced [collaboration features](https://www.flowlu.com/features/collaboration/)
    ? Make a list of what's important to you.
2.  **Explore Options:** Look into apps like Todoist, Flowlu, and Trello. Each offers unique features that might align better with your workflow.
3.  **Test Them Out:** Most of these apps offer free versions or trials. Give them a spin to see which feels right.
4.  **Consider the Learning Curve:** Some tools are more complex than others. Ensure that the time investment to learn a new system is worth the benefits it brings.

Remember, the best tool is the one that fits seamlessly into your routine and [enhances your productivity](https://www.flowlu.com/blog/flowlu-tips/12-tips-on-how-to-increase-your-flowlu-productivity/)
. It's worth taking the time to find the right match.

Best Task Management Solutions Beyond Things 3
----------------------------------------------

![Flowlu](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/31510/flowlu_4.png)

Flowlu

Your all-in-one work management solution

[Try For Free](https://my.flowlu.com/register/)

Flowlu brings together team collaboration, client and project management, document generation, knowledge sharing, mind maps, and financial tracking, all in one place. With Flowlu, you can automate those repetitive tasks that slow you down. Rely on real-time reporting to get insights into what’s working and where you can improve. Flowlu offers multiple task views—like Kanban, list, or calendar—so you can visualize work the way that suits your team best. With all your data in one place, making informed decisions is simpler than ever. It's all about reducing the effort of daily tasks, so you can focus on what really matters—growing your business and delivering value to your clients.

![Smartsheet](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/31514/smartsheet-1.png)

Smartsheet

Project tracking with a familiar spreadsheet touch

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/smartsheet/)

Smartsheet’s spreadsheet-style interface makes it easy to track projects, collaborate with teams, and automate workflows. From detailed Gantt charts to simple task lists, it adapts to fit any team’s needs. Manage everything from big-picture strategy to everyday tasks in one flexible workspace.

![ProofHub](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32067/proofhub-1.png)

ProofHub

Comprehensive project control

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/proofhub/)

ProofHub offers an all-in-one platform for project management and team collaboration. Plan, organize, and deliver projects efficiently while keeping communication centralized. With features like task timelines, online proofing, and built-in chat, your team stays connected and in sync. It’s a comprehensive solution that brings clarity and control to your workflows.

![Zoho One](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32068/zoho_one_2x.png)

Zoho One

Complete business suite

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/zoho-one/)

Zoho One is a full-fledged business platform with over 40 integrated apps, covering everything from sales to marketing to project management. It streamlines your operations by keeping all your tools under one login. Customize and scale the platform as your business grows, without worrying about the complexity of managing multiple systems.

![Trello](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32070/trello.png)

Trello

Agile team management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/trello/)

Trello’s classic boards and cards make it easy (and even a bit fun) to see your projects at a glance. Create, organize, and move tasks through your workflow with just a few clicks. Whether it's a personal to-do list or a team project, Trello keeps everything simple and right where you need it.

![Jira](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32071/jira.png)

Jira

Agile Project management for dev teams

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/jira/)

Jira is built for development teams that need powerful tools for tracking tasks, bugs, and features. It supports both Scrum and Kanban methodologies, offering deep customization and reporting features. Automate workflows and gain visibility into your project’s progress, all while integrating seamlessly with your existing tools.

![ActiveCollab](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32072/activcolab.png)

ActiveCollab

All-in-one tool for smart teams

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/activecollab/)

ActiveCollab helps you manage tasks, track time, and collaborate with your team all in one place. Its intuitive interface makes project management feel effortless, allowing you to focus on getting things done. From task assignments to invoicing clients, ActiveCollab covers every aspect of your workflow.

![WorkflowMax](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32073/workflowmax_2x.png)

WorkflowMax

Project lifecycle manager

WorkflowMax handles your entire project lifecycle, from lead generation to invoicing. Perfect for service-based businesses, it simplifies time tracking, task management, and financial reporting. With detailed insights into project profitability, you’ll always know where your business stands.

![Dubsado](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32074/dubsado_2x.png)

Dubsado

Client-centric project tool

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/dubsado/)

Dubsado combines CRM, project management, and invoicing in one powerful platform designed for client-facing businesses. From sending contracts to managing client communications, Dubsado automates routine tasks so you can focus on delivering great work. Its clean interface makes it easy to keep track of projects, schedules, and invoices all in one place.

![Favro](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32075/favro_2x-1.png)

Favro

Agile team collaboration

Favro helps teams adapt and scale with flexible workflows and real-time collaboration. Manage sprints, projects, and roadmaps with an intuitive platform that grows with your team. Track progress, adjust priorities, and collaborate across departments—all from one adaptable tool.

![Scoro](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32077/scoro_2x-1.png)

Scoro

Complete business management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/scoro/)

Scoro combines project management, billing, and reporting into one seamless experience. Manage your tasks, track time, and get real-time insights into project profitability with Scoro’s all-in-one platform. Running a creative agency or a professional service firm? Scoro helps you optimize every aspect of your business with ease.

![LiquidPlanner](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32078/liquidplanner_2x-1.png)

LiquidPlanner

Predictive project scheduling

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/liquidplanner-portfolio-manager/)

LiquidPlanner uses predictive scheduling to give teams a clear view of timelines and resources. It dynamically adjusts as priorities shift, ensuring your team stays focused on the most important tasks. The platform’s real-time forecasting tools let you manage uncertainty and make informed decisions.

![Basecamp](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32079/basecamp.png)

Basecamp

Simplified team collaboration

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/basecamp/)

Basecamp makes it easy for teams to collaborate by keeping everything—tasks, files, messages—organized in one place. With its clean interface and intuitive layout, you’ll never feel overwhelmed by clutter. It’s a straightforward tool for teams that need a simple way to stay in sync.

![Nifty](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32081/nifty.png)

Nifty

Real-time project tracker

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/nifty/)

Nifty combines task management, collaboration, and project timelines in one intuitive platform. Plan and track your projects from start to finish with real-time updates that keep everyone aligned. Nifty’s easy-to-use interface and automation features make it ideal for growing teams that need flexibility.

![ProjectLibre](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32083/projectlibre_2x-1.png)

ProjectLibre

Open-source project manager

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/projectlibre/)

ProjectLibre is a free, open-source alternative to expensive project management tools like Microsoft Project. With robust features like Gantt charts, task tracking, and resource management, it’s ideal for teams on a budget. ProjectLibre is powerful enough to handle complex projects but simple enough for everyday use.

![Bitrix24](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32084/bitrix24.png)

Bitrix24

Integrated business collaboration

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/bitrix24/)

Bitrix24 combines project management, communication, and CRM into a single, integrated platform. Manage tasks, collaborate with your team, and keep client information organized—all without leaving the app. Designed for small teams and large organizations alike, Bitrix24 offers the tools you need to streamline operations.

![TeamGantt](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32086/teamgantt_2x-1.png)

TeamGantt

Simple Gantt chart management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamgantt/)

TeamGantt simplifies project planning with its intuitive drag-and-drop Gantt chart interface. Visualize your tasks, set dependencies, and keep your team aligned on deadlines, all with minimal setup. It’s perfect for teams that want an easy way to track project progress without complicated software.

![Plutio](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32087/plutio_2x-1.png)

Plutio

Freelancer business management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/plutio/)

Plutio is built for freelancers and small teams who need an all-in-one solution to manage tasks, clients, and invoices. It offers everything you need to run your business seamlessly, including project tracking and communication, without the need to switch between tools. Customizable workflows allow you to manage projects your way, and integrated invoicing helps you get paid faster.

![Microsoft Project](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32088/ms_projects_2x-1.png)

Microsoft Project

Advanced project planning

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/microsoft-project/)

Microsoft Project is a comprehensive tool for planning, scheduling, and managing projects of any size. With its powerful Gantt charts, resource management tools, and reporting features, it’s the go-to platform for project managers worldwide. Customize your workflows, allocate resources, and track progress with real-time data.

![Redmine](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32090/redmine.png)

Redmine

Open-source task tracking

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/redmine/)

Redmine is a versatile, open-source project management tool designed for teams that need flexibility. Track issues, tasks, and timelines while integrating seamlessly with your favorite tools. Ideal for managing software development or running agile sprints, Redmine provides a customizable experience that adapts to your workflow.

![Planview](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32091/planview_2x-1.png)

Planview

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/planview-clarizen/)

Planview (former Clarizen) provides enterprise-grade project management with robust features designed for large-scale operations. Plan, execute, and deliver complex projects while gaining real-time insights into every aspect of your workflow.

![Accelo](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32092/accelo_2x-1.png)

Accelo

Service business automation

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/accelo/)

Accelo is built for service-based businesses that need to manage projects, clients, and billing all in one place. Track time, automate workflows, and keep projects on schedule, all while ensuring client satisfaction. Accelo integrates every part of your service delivery process, from lead generation to invoicing.

![Avaza](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32093/avaza_2x-1.png)

Avaza

Cloud-based team collaboration

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/avaza/)

Avaza brings together project management, team collaboration, and invoicing into a single, cloud-based platform. Organize tasks, track time, and manage client communications without the need for separate tools. Its clean interface and powerful features make it a great choice for teams seeking to simplify workflows and boost productivity.

![Paymo](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32094/paymo_2x-1.png)

Paymo

Time tracking with billing

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/paymo/)

Paymo is designed for teams and freelancers who need to manage projects, track time, and handle billing from one central platform. It’s perfect for businesses that want a seamless way to stay on top of tasks, deadlines, and client invoicing. Paymo’s real-time tracking and reporting give you full visibility into project progress and profitability. It’s an all-in-one solution for those who want to manage work and payments effortlessly.

![HoneyBook](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32095/honeybook_2x-1.png)

HoneyBook

Client process automation

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/honeybook/)

HoneyBook offers a complete client management solution for freelancers and small businesses. From proposals and contracts to payments, it helps you streamline your entire client process. Its automation features take the hassle out of onboarding and invoicing, so you can focus on delivering great work.

![YouTrack](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32096/youtrack_2x-1.png)

YouTrack

Agile development tracking

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/youtrack/)

YouTrack offers an agile project management experience tailored for development teams. Track tasks, bugs, and sprints with customizable workflows and advanced reporting features. Its powerful search capabilities and real-time updates keep your team on top of every task.

![Asana](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32097/asana.png)

Asana

Workflows made simple

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/asana/)

Asana is a project management tool that helps teams stay organized and keep track of tasks. It’s great for planning, assigning responsibilities, and visualizing project timelines with a simple interface. With features like boards, lists, and integrations, Asana makes managing both small and large projects very straightforward.

![Wrike](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32098/wrike.png)

Wrike

Real-time team alignment

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/wrike/)

Wrike is built for teams needing detailed visibility into their projects. It offers tools for managing tasks, tracking time, and collaborating in real time, all from one platform. Wrike’s customizable dashboards and reporting make it easy to keep everyone aligned and on top of deadlines.

![Monday](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32099/mondey.png)

Monday

Visual task management

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/monday-com/)

Monday is a visual project management tool that adapts to your team's workflow. It lets you track projects, set up automations, and manage everything from daily tasks to big projects on a colorful, user-friendly interface. Its flexibility makes it ideal for teams of all sizes looking to streamline processes.

![ClickUp](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32100/clickup.png)

ClickUp

Customizable productivity hub

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/clickup/)

ClickUp is an all-in-one productivity platform where you can manage tasks, docs, goals, and more. It’s designed to replace multiple apps by combining project management, communication, and automation in one place. Customizable views and extensive integrations help teams tailor their workspace to fit their requirements.

![Teamwork](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/32101/teamwork_2x-1.png)

Teamwork

End-to-end project tracking

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/teamwork/)

Teamwork is a project management platform designed to streamline team collaboration. It allows companies to manage tasks, track time, and stay on top of project timelines with ease. With its client-focused features, Teamwork makes managing complex projects and keeping everyone aligned simple.

![Sunsama](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35726/sunsama.png)

Sunsama

Plan With Purpose

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/sunsama/)

Sunsama blends task management with daily planning, helping you timebox your work and actually stick to a realistic schedule. It’s built for deep work, meaning fewer distractions and more meaningful progress—not just checking boxes.

![Todoist](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35727/todoist.png)

Todoist

Simple, Powerful, Organized

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/todoist/)

Todoist is simple, fast, and uncluttered, making it perfect for keeping track of everything from your grocery list to your next big project. With powerful filters, recurring tasks, and team collaboration, it scales with you—whether you're running a business or just trying to remember to water your plants.

![MeisterTask](https://www.flowlu.com/site/assets/files/35729/meistertask_2x.png)

MeisterTask

Visual Workflow, Effortless Execution

[](https://www.flowlu.com/alternative/meistertask/)

MeisterTask brings a Kanban-style workflow to life with automation, intuitive UI, and real-time collaboration. Perfect for teams that need structure without the complexity, it turns work into a smooth, colorful process—because let’s be honest, project management should feel less like a chore.

FAQs

See the most answers to the most frequently asked questions. You can find even more information in the knowledge base.

[Knowledge base](https://www.flowlu.com/help/)

Can Flowlu replace Things 3?

Yes, Flowlu can serve as an alternative to Things 3. While Things 3 is primarily a task management app for individual use, Flowlu offers a comprehensive suite of tools, including project management, CRM, and financial management, making it suitable for both personal and team-based tasks.

What are the top alternatives to Things 3 for Windows and Android users?

Since Things 3 is exclusive to Apple devices, Windows and Android users often look for comparable task management tools. Popular alternatives include Todoist, TickTick, and Microsoft To Do. These apps offer cross-platform support and a range of features that cater to various productivity needs.

Are there any free alternatives to Things 3?

Yes, there are free alternatives to Things 3. Google Tasks is a notable option, offering seamless integration with Gmail and Google Calendar. It's entirely free and allows you to manage tasks across all devices. Other free options include Flowlu and Microsoft To Do, both of which provide robust task management features without any cost.
